Alumnus here. Anyone recommend a good therapist for hopelessness? Perhaps depression? I've been working for more than a decade now, after a course I didn't pick. I struggled throughout my college years as I simply lacked the motivation and interest to get through my subjects, no matter how much I tried. Right now I'm in an industry I absolutely have no passion for, working jobs for measly pay and abysmal growth. To make things even more desperate, my industry is dying and I know my time is running out. I tried over the years to improve my life and pursue the work I actually am passionate for, but due to a wide variety of factors I could never successfully do so. If they allowed me to choose what I wanted, I know I would be in a much better position to live. How do I deal with this? Its been over a decade now, and I'm still grappling with the consequences of a choice I had no say in. What I wanted never mattered to them, and I'm the one paying the price. I'm constantly compared to my far more successful cousins, and I know now I'd never amount to even a shadow of their achievements. I still have some plans left to save my future but I'm currently starving, broke, lonely, and hopeless. I'm sitting out here on the sidewalk as I type this, just clocking out of soul-crushing work that lasts from early mornings to midnight everyday and counting the days until I'm finally laid off. So any recommendations? I feel I need professional help. I just wanted to pursue my passion, the same way my cousins pursued theirs and were well-supported in turn no matter the expenses. I just wanted to live knowing my choices fucking mattered. Why does no one else want that for me?

Hi admin can you post this po? Hello everyone, we are kindly asking for help for our friend who recently got into a motorcycle accident. He was trying to overtake when another motorcycle also attempted to overtake at the same time, which caused both of them to lose balance. Unfortunately, the incident resulted in injuries and the motorcycle also hit an elderly person nearby. We understand that mistakes were made, and our friend deeply regrets what happened. Right now, he is facing medical expenses and other bills related to the accident, which have become very overwhelming for him and his family. Because of this, we are reaching out to anyone with a kind heart who may be willing to help, even in the smallest way. Any amount you can share will truly mean a lot and will go directly toward hospital bills, medication, and assistance to the elderly who was hit during the incident. Every peso counts and will help ease the burden during this difficult time. For those who would like to help, we can provide proof of the hospital bills and other expenses. It must be stated, at the outset, that entering into any form of personal relationship carries with it an implied obligation of good faith—most critically, the duty to communicate with clarity, consistency, and honesty. When an individual repeatedly fails to meet this standard, particularly by saying one thing and doing another, such conduct reflects not mere oversight, but a pattern of unreliability. In the case at hand, the issue is not rooted in complexity, but in the evident inability—or unwillingness—to “walk the talk.” Assurances are given, expectations are set, yet follow-through remains conspicuously absent. Communication, when it does occur, is inconsistent, ambiguous, or conveniently timed, leaving the other party in a state of uncertainty. This is not miscommunication; it is a failure to communicate responsibly. Let it be clear: effective communication is not an optional courtesy—it is a fundamental requirement. Without it, any form of connection is inherently unstable. Resorting to excuses, deflections, or vague justifications only exacerbates the issue and reflects poorly on one’s character and intent. At a certain point, such behavior ceases to be accidental and becomes indicative of a disregard for the other party’s time and emotional investment. Accordingly, if an individual lacks the capacity to communicate properly, the appropriate course of action is not to persist in half-formed commitments, but to refrain from initiating them altogether. Engaging in something one cannot sustain is, in essence, creating unnecessary harm under the guise of participation. Finally, it bears emphasizing that actions carry consequences. Patterns of inconsistency and poor communication do not exist in isolation; they return in kind. What goes around, indeed, comes around. In sum, if one cannot communicate with clarity, stand by their word, and act with consistency, then one should cease making excuses and reconsider their participation altogether. Anything less is, simply put, untenable.

Sometimes, what we think is good for our local communities might not necessarily translate to being good on a national and global scale. Exhibit A? The [REDACTED_NAME] admin’s draconian decision to shut down the entire campus during “digital days”. Seriously? Yes, we can say that [NCR] as an institution has done its part in this energy crisis. But what happens when teachers can no longer work in a single SHARED faculty office? What happens when students can no longer study in a more PUBLIC space like the Rizal Library? What happens is that THESE INDIVIDUALS TURN ON THEIR AIRCON UNITS AT HOME. (Can you blame them?) So essentially, instead of a lot of faculty sharing one single airconditioned office on campus, you now have MULTIPLE aircons running because people now have to turn them on when they’re at home... The burden was taken off [NCR]’s hands — and the credit goes to [NCR] as a whole for being this “globally-responsible”, “environmentally-friendly” institution. But the burden gets passed down to the individual constituents that make up [NCR] as a community. And does the environment end up winning if all these people now stay at home and turn their aircon units on? Make it make sense, admin! Someone make it make sense!

Over the past few years that's I've done org work, both as a former finance officer and a finance core team member, I've had a realization that a lot orgs do NOT understand what the job of finance actually is. To be fair it's very difficult to make the distinction unless you yourself are in finance, but I want to set the record straight. Finance's job is all about making transactions and recording those transactions. Before you comment, hear me out first. What does this imply exactly? Well it means that if I'm in a finance core team, I shouldn't be expected to ask a company for a quotation or rates, that's a job for logistics. What I AM expected to do is to check if the provided quotation aligns with the project's budget, make adjustments if necessary (or even help look for alternatives), and process the payment for whatever it is was purchased. This is why in practice, finance core teams are generally unnecessary unless it's for a big event where a large amount of transactions are happening at the same time. Otherwise, if it's just for purchasing of some materials, you might as well get the Finance Officer or their AVPs to do the job instead, ESPECIALLY when usage of the DCB can only be approved by the President or Finance Officer. You wouldn't trust a random core team member with approvals for reimbursements would you? I would hope not. This sounds like such a nothing issue to most people but being finance in orgs with the current incorrect perception of finance causes either those in finance core teams to have absolutely nothing to do until the very last minute and possibly doing a job that is not in their job description, or the finance officer and/or their AVPs to be severely overworked and overwhelmed. Suffice to say, please have mercy on your finance people.

Dear [NCR] and Filipino undergrads, Oh, if only you knew! [REDACTED_NAME], [NCR] ranks, what, something. Go to actual top universities in the world and you’ll notice so much differences. 1) Writing a thesis To be able to graduate from I believe any undergraduate program here in the Philippines, you need to write a thesis. And usually totaling to 70–100 pages. Look at your American counterparts from Harvard, Yale, etc. The ACTUAL top universities worldwide. They might NOT even be required to WRITE a thesis! Why? I can think of at least two reasons. First, academia in the Philippines is rigid. I can honestly see traces of the “I went through this, therefore you must as well” mentality among professors and academic requirements here. Academia in the Philippines wants your blood, not just your sweat, and that’s the key to success, so that your diploma is marred by your own blood. As mentioned by comments in a previous FW post, there are MASTER’S DEGREES programs from the top universities abroad that do NOT require writing a master’s thesis. And you have to write an undergrad one at 100 pages, academia draining your blood and making you tired just to uphold rigid old structures. 2) Master’s and doctoral degrees. This is a BIG one. In the US, again in some of the top universities in the whole world, some doctoral programs (PhD) allow you NOT to have a master’s degree before enrolling. The key here is that you take around five years to complete the doctoral degree, and perhaps in your third year of doctoral study, you will have earned a master’s degree already in the process. YES. You read this right. Do a quick research, and some of the top universities in the entire world allow you to go from undergrad STRAIGHT INTO a PhD program. Here in the Philippines, again, it’s sweat and blood. Master’s degree for 5 years. Doctoral degrees for 7 years. Here in the Philippines, the name of the game is not about wanting students to succeed efficiently. It’s about burdening them with unnecessary requirements that make it harder for students to succeed. I hear.. [NCR] is being investigated by international bodies because so few people have been graduating from PhD programs there. Hmmm. Meanwhile, a 30 year old successfully finishes his or her PhD from Harvard University — 30 YEARS OLD. Harvard University! What a joke we are compared to the great universities worldwide!

Small rant about pursuing law: I think the main reason I’m pulling away from law is because I really want to leave home. My grandparents won’t let me live on my own, not even in an apartment or a condo. Even if [REDACTED_NAME] is far, I still come home every night. I feel like I haven’t really been allowed to go anywhere, to have a life that’s mine. I’ve always been someone who leaves. When something doesn’t feel right anymore, I walk away. I’ve done it with places, with people, with versions of myself that I outgrew. We used to live in the province before moving to Manila. And right now, all I want is to go somewhere far, maybe abroad now, and build a life on my own, even if it’s temporary, even if I’m still figuring things out. The thought of staying in law school for four more years, still studying, while my friends move on into stable jobs, scares me more than I can explain. I don’t want to feel left behind. I don’t want to feel like my life is on hold while everyone else is already living theirs. I still want to become an attorney. I want to do it for security. If I have this title, no one can hurt me. That ambition is still there. But it doesn’t feel as strong as this need to live, to leave, to finally choose something for myself. And I think, right now, I want a life more than I want the title. Any advice, please? Do I still go for law?

Cross posting from Reddit to gain more awareness BEWARE: Soap Selling "Scam" TLDR: Two individuals **allegedly** from Phinma Saint Jude tried selling me soap outside of Gate 1. Looking online, this seems to be a common scam targeting students who may find it difficult to say no. Yesterday I was approached by two students, who allegedly are from Phinma Saint Jude outside of Gate 1. They asked me if I had time to spare as they were looking for people to talk to for a public speaking/marketing class, and that they were econ students. I was kind of surprised and just agreed. At first, it was small talk, and they asked what year I was, course, etc. which to me, seemed pretty believable for public speaking. But then, they brought up how they were selling soap for their course, how it was FDA approved (which they pulled up a random picture from their phone with documents, I cannot confirm if it's a legitimate FDA approval), how it contains niacinamide and other good ingredients for the skin, and how proceeds would be donated to charity (which they then pulled out another set of pictures to show their "beneficiaries"). It struck me as odd and I had an unsettling feeling that something was not right. Largely because they told me it was a public speaking requirement but now they're marketing soap? The soap was a nondescript generic white looking soap with no logo or ingredients, and was wrapped in very thin cheap plastic (think of the plastic covering sticker sheets). They told me they've sold soap in multiple places already ([NCR], [NCR], etc.) and even pulled up pictures with [REDACTED_NAME] and other University students who had bought from them prior to them approaching me. I feel they were trying to gain my trust from multiple angles as they established common ground (being uni students having to complete a req), donating to a charity, FDA approval,showing other students bought, etc., and they were overall very friendly. They even made off hand comments of how their research won't fail because it wasn't raining and how they had to return to campus by ~6PM. Looking online, this seems to be a common scam that happens outside universities. And while I'm not 100 percent sure they are scammers, I hope you guys learn from me and be more aware and careful of people approaching you guys randomly. I don't know if guards/admin knows or if they'd necessarily care about it since it's technically outside campus.

hello just sharing [REDACTED_NAME] i wrote :>> where perfection is placed on a pedestal and where being “enough” is somehow still inadequate, how should one stand? how does one position themselves between two extremes, where the only acceptable standard seems to equate to flawlessness? this rigid binary—between the good and the bad, the excellent and mediocre—baffles me. we are almost always said to exist somewhere along this spectrum, yet what they never tell us is that we can fluctuate between different points, or even occupy several of them at once. i find myself grappling with this paradox, because in the pursuit of perfection—in the pursuit of being “enough”—i fall short, even when i have supposedly reached the goal. how strange, because in moments of achievement, it is somehow both too much and not enough. and even in my relationships, why is it that i often feel at risk of being “too”—too intense, too loud, too understanding—where being “too” implies excess, and excess implies fault? thus, we are told to strike a balance, to be “just enough,” yet even that idea feels unsettling. is it truly enough to simply be “enough?” how, then, does one know when to speak and when to remain silent? how do we tell if our “too much” is someone else’s “not enough?” perhaps this uncertainty is an inevitable part of our personhood—perhaps to be human is to live in these in-betweens, reminding ourselves that there is growth in the fluctuations, in not completely knowing where to stand. in the process, i am slowly learning that it is acceptable to strive for“enough,” because the standard was never fixed to begin with—perhaps it was just a word i have come to associate with other people’s versions of “enough.”

The moment [REDACTED_NAME] decided to give free ice cream and free photobooth as an incentive STRICTLY FOR SHARING about the gen elec, it already guaranteed a failure for this semester’s elections. Sure, you want people to promote the idea of voting, but what then if it remains there? People will just share, share, and share, because that’s the main goal. Right? Nobody will take elections seriously, because the student government itself isn’t taken seriously. Some people will understand your motive through encouraging the promotion of elections, HOWEVER, there will come a point when people actually need to act. What about giving better incentives for the ones who actually voted? Put the free photobooth and ice cream as incentives under this, if that’s what you want so bad. It’s not like you’re forcing them to vote, but rather conditioning the students through instant gratification that voting will result in a positive outcome (free stuff, yay). Despite having the most students, I’m not surprised [DEPARTMENT] stands last place for voter turnout; they know they’ll be the ones to rule the country in the future anyway, because money and corporate runs everything. So [REDACTED_NAME], if by some miracle you find a way to wake these [DEPARTMENT] students up and have them rank higher than SOSS in the next voter turnout, I can guarantee you that people will take you seriously.

A great Realisation again from the Lord 😇🤍 What if [REDACTED_NAME] na you thought breaks you... Is what actually heals you? Let me share my testimony inspired by [REDACTED_NAME] and [REDACTED_NAME]🤍. Nagsimula po Ito nung binabasa the Gospel of Matthew. Habang patuloy kong binabasa [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] in [REDACTED_NAME], and I stopped at the words of the Lord [REDACTED_NAME]: > “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. Go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’” That phrase — “I desire mercy, not sacrifice” — echoed deep inside me. Naging sobra curious ako, wondering Kung saan Ito makikita sa Scripture. And then I searched for it and when I searched, I found it in Hosea 6:6, and so I opened the Book of Hosea — not knowing that what I was about to read would cut and heal me at the same time. As I read through [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] how God’s people turned away from Him. Ang winoworship nila ay mga idols like [REDACTED_NAME] and gave themselves to false gods, believing na makahanap sila [REDACTED_NAME] joy at fulfillment doon. At kahit na pinagpatuloy nila mga pagsasacrifice at mga religious practices, [REDACTED_NAME] unti unting lumalayo [REDACTED_NAME]. And then it hit me: [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME]. The Lord was reminding them — pati tayo— na what God truly wants isn’t outward religion, but inward devotion. Hindi performance, kundi love. Hindi sacrifice, kundi mercy. In that moment, na realized [REDACTED_NAME]’s desire has always been the same: He wants our hearts 🥹🤍 As I kept reading [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] the depth of His sorrow — at ang kanyang pagmamahal sakin. He spoke of tearing and cutting, of allowing pain and discipline. [REDACTED_NAME], those verses sounded like anger, but then [REDACTED_NAME] said, “I will be like a lion,” and “My word will cut them to pieces.” I paused. That word “like” told me something — this was a symbolic cutting, a spiritual wound. And then it became clear: His Word pierces not flesh, but the heart. It was then that I realized — God’s Word cuts not to destroy, but to heal. It exposes sin, pride, and self-deception so deeply that it feels like being torn apart from the inside. But that pain isn’t punishment; it’s correction wrapped in mercy. It’s the kind of pain that brings awakening and healing. As [REDACTED_NAME] says: > “Come, let us return to the Lord. He has torn us to pieces but He will heal us; He has injured us but He will bind up our wounds.” That verse became my story. I began to see how everything God allowed in my life — every heartbreak, every loss, every tear — had a purpose. He was doing the same with me as He did with Israel: allowing pain not to punish me, but to discipline me and lead me back to His heart. I understood now why my past relationships ended. I thought they were beautiful and full of love, but in truth, they were mixed with human desires and not with God’s will. What I called “love” was often filled with impurity — and when it ended, I thought God was being cruel. But now I see He was being merciful. He said in [REDACTED_NAME] 2:6: > “I will fence her with thornbushes; I will block her path with a wall so that she loses her way.” And suddenly I understood — when God blocks your path, He’s not trying to destroy your happiness. He’s protecting your soul. He allows you to lose what you thought you needed, so that you can find what you truly need — Him. Then God whispered a wisdom into my heart: > “The thing you thought breaks you is what actually heals you.” I realized that the true brokenness is not the pain of loss, but the pain of seeing the truth about your sin. The deepest wound comes when you finally see how far you’ve gone from God — and yet that’s where real healing begins. When I compared the pain of heartbreak to the pain of realizing my own sin, I found that the second one cut much deeper. Because losing someone hurts for a while, but realizing how I’ve hurt God breaks the soul. Still, that brokenness became sacred — because that’s when [REDACTED_NAME] met me with grace. When He saved me, I felt a peace that no relationship, no pleasure, no success could ever give. I felt the love of a Father — not one who tolerates sin, but one who corrects with care. > “For the Lord disciplines those He loves.” — Hebrews 12:6 That’s when I realized: only God can both correct and heal. He’s the only One whose words pierce like a double-edged sword, yet bind the wound tighter than before. That’s why the enemy hates Him so much. That’s why Christianity is constantly attacked — because it’s not a religion of hiding sin, but of exposing it and finding redemption in the light. [REDACTED_NAME] can’t stand that kind of truth because it sets people free. But even then, I found another layer of wisdom — something that even wise men of the world say: > “Knowing your weakness is what frees you from it.” That’s the beauty of God’s love. He doesn’t just forgive our weaknesses — He teaches us to face them, so that His grace can replace them. Like [REDACTED_NAME] said, “When I am weak, then I am strong.” So now I live not to hide my past, but to glorify the God who turned my pain into purpose. I no longer fear His correction; I thank Him for it — because I’ve learned that His discipline is proof of His love. And now, every time I open His Word, I remember: > It pierces like a sword — but also heals deeper than the wound it caused. It humbles me — but also lifts me higher than I could ever reach on my own. So praise and glory be to God Almighty — my Father, my Healer, my Redeemer. For truly, it is only in Him that correction and healing become one. 🙌🏼🤍

Vista 309 [NCR] = Worst Condo If you are considering renting or buying a unit at Vista 309 [NCR], I strongly advise you to think twice. My experience with the building administration has been extremely frustrating due to their lack of responsiveness, professionalism, and basic concern for residents. As a resident, I make it a point to pay my rent, utilities, and condo dues on time. In return, I expect at the very least basic building management and support when issues arise. Unfortunately, that has not been the case. Recently, the building experienced a power outage, and the response from the administration was incredibly disappointing. Despite residents being left without electricity, the admin office showed little urgency or concern in addressing the situation. They were unaccommodating and dismissive, providing minimal information and showing no clear effort to resolve the issue promptly. Electricity is a basic necessity, and situations like this require immediate attention and communication from management. Instead, residents were left without clear updates or assistance, even though we consistently fulfill our financial obligations to the building. It is extremely frustrating to diligently pay bills and association dues, only to receive such poor service when problems occur. Residents deserve responsive management, transparency, and accountability—none of which were demonstrated in this situation. If you are looking for a condo in [NCR], I recommend carefully considering other options before choosing Vista 309.

A Day in the Life of a Trentahin [REDACTED_DEMONYM] 6:00 AM — The Vertical Life He wakes up in a one- or two-bedroom condo in BGC, Rockwell, or Ortigas. The aircon hums softly. His blackout curtains filter in the hazy Manila light. From the 28th floor, traffic already looks dense. His phone is his alarm — iPhone, latest or near-latest model. Before getting out of bed, he scrolls: • Viber work chats (already active) • Slack notifications • News about the peso, traffic, or global markets • Instagram stories from friends in Singapore or New York He works in finance, tech, consulting, law, FMCG, or a multinational. Salary: strong six figures monthly, maybe more with bonuses. [REDACTED_NAME] or [REDACTED_NAME] — possibly with a master’s abroad. He showers quickly. Skincare is basic but deliberate. He owns a few good watches but rotates between Apple Watch and a mechanical piece depending on the meeting calendar. Breakfast is either: • Black coffee from his Nespresso machine • Or a quick stop at Starbucks, Pickup Coffee, or a third-wave café downstairs If he’s being “healthy,” it’s overnight oats or a protein shake. 7:30 AM — The Commute Equation He either: • Drives a compact SUV (Ford Territory, Montero, CR-V, maybe a Lexus if he’s further along), or • Uses Grab Premium to avoid parking stress. He times his departure carefully. Manila traffic is not an inconvenience; it is a force of nature to be strategically navigated. Podcasts during the drive: • Business or investing • Self-improvement • Sometimes a true crime series If coding or finance-heavy, he might review decks on his iPad in the car. 8:30 AM — Corporate Glass Towers He badges into a modern office tower. His workspace: • Dual monitors • Noise-canceling headphones • A [REDACTED_NAME] chair (if the company invested well) His English is polished. His Tagalog is fluent but often mixed — effortless code-switching: “Can you send the revised deck before lunch? [REDACTED_NAME]to today.” Meetings dominate: • Regional calls with Singapore • Clients dialing in from Makati or Hong Kong • Budget reviews • Strategy sessions He is ambitious but measured. He wants director level by mid-30s. He tracks peers quietly — who got promoted, who moved to Dubai, who left for Australia. 12:30 PM — Lunch as Networking Lunch is rarely random. Options: • Sweetgreen-style salad places • Japanese set meals • Power lunch at Wildflour or Mendokoro • Company cafeteria if pressed for time He eats with: • Work friends (same schools, similar backgrounds) • A senior he’s trying to build rapport with • Or alone, scrolling LinkedIn Topics: • Property investments in Nuvali or Alabang • Crypto (more cautiously than in ) • Who’s getting married next • Whether to migrate or stay There’s always an undercurrent of comparison — salary bands, condos purchased, career velocity. 3:00 PM — The Grind Afternoon slump. Coffee . He’s juggling: • Deadlines • Office politics • Regional expectations • Deliverables that are “urgent” but were avoidable He is competent. High-functioning. Reliable. He knows how to present confidently even when unsure. He feels pressure — not survival pressure, but performance pressure. 6:30 PM — The Split Path After work, his life branches. Path A: The Gym Professional • Fitness First, Anytime Fitness, or a boutique gym • Push-pull-legs routine • Protein shake after • Gym selfies occasionally, but subtle Health is part vanity, part discipline, part stress relief. Path B: Social • Dinner in Poblacion, BGC High Street, or Molito • Craft beer, cocktails, or wine • Conversations about startups, travel, politics He dates through: • Bumble • Mutual friends • [NCR] networks Many women in his circle are equally educated and career-driven. 9:30 PM — Condo Quiet Back home. Shoes off. Aircon on. City lights outside. He might: • Watch Netflix or NBA • Play PS5 • Review investments (stocks, MP2, maybe a small condo rental) • Scroll endlessly He sends money home monthly — even if his family is comfortable. It’s partly cultural, partly pride. The Internal Landscape Externally, he is successful. Internally, he cycles through: • Ambition: “Am I moving fast enough?” • Comparison: “[REDACTED_NAME].” • Migration thought: “Should I try for Canada? Or is staying smarter?” • Family pressure: Marriage by early-to-mid 30s. • Financial anxiety: Lifestyle inflation vs long-term security. He is global in mindset but deeply Filipino in obligations. He believes in meritocracy — but also knows connections matter. He is comfortable — but not relaxed.

Prof here. Since the issue of faculty salary has been posted in the past, a post which received much pushback, let’s also talk about what profs can do that people from other careers can’t: 1) Leisure of time. If you’re in corporate, you’re 9am–5pm or 6pm. Period. You have no choice. On the other hand, profs need not come to school unless they have class. And they choose the timeslots they want. Some wake up at 8am to start their 11am classes. A regular corporate day will never see something like that. It is quite common to see profs in their offices just talking to one another because their work/classes for the day have been completed. 2) Days off and salary. Profs do not get paid less if class is suspended because of weather. The salary is the same, even if class suspensions last for 1 whole week. (Not a great thing of course, because that means something bad is happening in the country.) In [NCR], profs also have the option to dedicate a certain number of hours for asynchronous activities in lieu of meeting onsite. We don’t get paid less for that; the salary stays the same. And yes, these asynchronous days are needed because some content is more reading heavy than others. All those holidays? Chinese New Year, QC Day, President’s Day? Salary is the same. 3) Travel and conference incentives. [NCR] allots a certain amount of money to sponsor the academic-related travels of its faculty members. Yes, even international travel. If the faculty’s expenses are not too high, then the money [NCR] gives to its faculty might even cover their entire international trip. Including airfare and hotel. Because of this, profs get to travel to places they may never even have wanted to go - because conferences are held in those places. This is needed because faculty need to expand their depth of knowledge and experience. And [NCR] could do well to support these even more, with less limit. 4) Tuition benefits. According to the [NCR] faculty handbook, an [NCR] prof (both full-time and part-time) puts their children through school in [NCR], the child’s tuition is discounted. Whether how much is discounted depends on whether the prof is full or part-time, but an amount is still an amount. Again, [NCR] could do well to support and raise this even more, though. Further: if a faculty member is applying for a graduate degree for him or herself here in [NCR], the tuition also gets discounted. Some support might also be had for studies outside [NCR] and even studies abroad. 5) Connections. Lots of it. Life in the academe can brings opportunities that range from guest lecturing in other schools (which earns them money) to maybe even consultancy for large entities. Watch the news, and you’ll even see profs being interviewed for this or that issue. Schools are essential knowledge powerhouses, and where will people find resource people? From a university. The point? [NCR] profs don’t eat from the trash. The salary might be lower than corporate, but the benefits could be very helpful if used wisely. Fight whatay be injustices, definitely. But alongside that, perhaps we can also be grateful for the good that already is.

short confession/rant on org relationships im in a lot of orgs. i hold positions and join projects as core/head in those orgs. i participate in their events, go to the tambays, hang in the org rooms. but why do i still end up--alone? i can't even be mad, the people i work and interact with have been so nice and accommodating. it's not like we don't talk either, it's just that we talk within--confines? within the org room, the department meeting, the event. but beyond the project offboarding and the sem-end parties that should be a space for connection, i dont really form a close bond with any of them. i keep thinking, am i too hopeful? maybe this is just org culture where everyone is a friend, a healthy mix of relational and transactional. it was never meant to be more than that. but then, i see people actually bond with their org friends and form genuine relationships. then i think, is it the org? i'm currently in a mix of advocacy-driven, career-oriented, and interest-based orgs, so if it really is the culture then, what orgs? and what stops the experience of entering the org, of doing so much and of meeting people just to be met with connection that doesn't go beyond the surface from happening? for those who reached the end and perhaps even resonated with this post, thank you. unfortunately though, for now, there's no conclusion to this. and for those whose spirits i've brought down, i'm so sorry--but i do still believe that we'll be alright :)

Misogyny and Misandry Two ends of the spectrum, the hatred of men versus the hatred of women. I was speaking with my brother and every once in a while he’d snap and call my feminism annoying. He hates when I enjoy BL because I like it when the love interests are inherently equal. If they had different statuses, it was because of the effects of their actions rather than it being ingrained in their society. He hates when I send “Man Hater” reels when it was something that just said, and I quote, “Women hate men as a system. Men hate women as individuals”. It breaks me a little that he could be so hateful over something that doesn’t affect him. When women fight for gender equality, we’re fighting to not be raped and/or killed. We’re fighting to not just be sexualized and then shamed for our sexuality. We’re fighting for all the opportunities that we weren’t given— simply for being born a girl. Men are fighting for “who pays for the first date”. I must admit, when I think I’m right, I would never bend my beliefs. I probably did annoy him. But, he sincerely doesn’t see the issue with the way he thinks. His belief that “he isn’t part of the issue so it therefore does not exist” disgusts me. I feel like I failed. Not just him, or my little sister, or me— but I feel like I failed his future partners and potential children. His future daughter becoming the target of his conceited way of thinking while his sons would agree and take on his beliefs. Never did I once think that women are better than men for simply being born a girl. Additionally, never did I think that men were inherently bad. My cautionary attitude towards men came from being made uncomfortable, harassed, stalked, and so much more by men who didn’t (and still don’t) understand boundaries. I don’t identify with misandry but I truly believe that it exists because of the way men have treated women. It exists because misogyny.

(I got the original text and made ai regenerate it for the sake of anonymity I’m a SOSS, and I am a victim of sexual harassment. The strange part is that when I finally came out about it, I also lost friends. This guy, let’s call him JT, had been a close friend of mine for around two years, and I genuinely valued that friendship. At one point he opened up to me about being outcasted during senior high, and later he came out to me as gay. None of that changed how I saw him, I still just saw him as JT. But over time, he started crossing boundaries. He became more physically intrusive, touching me more often and leaning on me without my consent. At first I brushed it off, but it got to the point where he would not take no for an answer. Whenever I confronted him, he would stop for a week, then go back to doing it again. What made it worse was that he also started venting about me to our other friends, supposedly out of “care” or “endearment,” but really just dissing me behind my back. He did the same with my girlfriend. He got close to her, followed the same pattern, and eventually she started joining in, bonding with him over my flaws, treating me badly when he was around, and piling onto me with the others. It felt like they were all making fun of me, bullying me, and slowly ruining my peace, while nobody stopped to think that the constant touching, intrusion of personal space, and humiliation were affecting me. Naturally, I became more emotionally unstable. I was crashing out more, losing restraint, and constantly feeling terrible, but no one seemed to ask why. Instead, I was just painted as the problem. It reached a particularly low point during my girlfriend’s party. He was touching me again in ways that made me visibly uncomfortable, to the point that even her friends could see it, but my own “friends” just kept encouraging it. When I finally pushed him off me, especially after we had already talked about him not touching me, I was somehow made out to be the bad guy for not treating my “best friend” properly. This went on for the whole year until I finally broke during a theology presentation. By that point, I had already attempted suicide three times. I felt like I could not breathe in school anymore. I told my girlfriend everything, ghosted everyone, and took two days off because I genuinely could not handle going back. When I returned, JT had already told people that he was scared of me, that what he did was just an accident, and that he deserved sympathy for what I had supposedly put him through. He shifted the blame onto me, and people believed him. I lost friends because of it. Even when I opened up to someone I trusted, let’s call her DA, who is active in orgs like APAIR, she responded passively aggressively, and from what I later learned, she even screenshotted my messages and sent them to JT. So not only did I go through the harassment itself, but when I finally spoke up about it, I was blamed, misrepresented, and abandoned by people I thought I could trust. I guess you could say that I ended up losing a lot, but in return, I gained my sanity back, and that’s something I’ll always choose. I’m sharing this not just for my own closure, but also to spread awareness for those going through the same thing. It’s okay to speak up. It’s okay to tell people. To anyone who has experienced this, your voice, your happiness, and your well-being should never be compromised by anyone else’s actions. Please speak up, and don’t let them or the people who side with them speak for you. You deserve to be heard, and you deserve better.

It's been 3 years now, and still trying to keep going, but it gets difficult nowadays. I did my best to improve my social skills and talents, even breaking my physical limits, but it still hinders me from seeing the realistically beautiful thing in life: to be accepted without prejudice, pity, and most importantly, deceit (or lying). Now, I am facing life as extremely draining and life-depleting, even beyond the hopes of the dating scene. "Is there something wrong with me?" I am the person who always gaslights and becomes their own imposter. The demand of life "to be myself" is difficult for people expect me to become." People cannot ask that because I ignore all the negative feedback (which I am emotionally sensitive to) and rumors that talk about me. In light of recent events, the potency of insecurity just pounded my wandering soul. "Am I wrong to hope for something fighting for?" The world is against me. I thank people who are kind to me; however, I was never kind. Being the "good [REDACTED_NAME]" or the "genuine" person who thinks of me is the only way I mask. The more I hide myself with this masking, the more I get exhausted and physically deteriorated. Sometimes, it's hard to believe what the world tells me to do: how to get myself up, and even how to socialize, is always demanded of me. But I never tell people that every choice I make is a limbo situation, where every choice counts. Most especially at the [REDACTED_NAME] of time, it cannot be wasted. Yes, it's hard to be a neurodivergent (autistic) person who does things for survival, not just for him to fit in, but also everything is fighting against me. What if the ones who supported me were not there anymore? What can I do? People tell me, "You will never have children or a family of your own." "You'll never get married." "You'll never find a purpose in life." Worst one: "Someone had prayed over you to never exist and never go out of life," and even told it to my family straightforwardly. I know people will blabber and nag that this person writing is trauma dumping or ranting about his life, that no one will bother. I mean, to ask you back: "Have you ever suffered to a incurable condition that you carried all over your life and continuing with newer comorbidities?" I guess my life is a total contradiction, paradox, ambivalence, and nightmare that people will ignore and take away the life out of you. The way I am doing right now is to value those who have cherished, supported, and loved me along the way. Despite masking, they'll know one day, and they truly will --- and once they find out, they will reflect (hopefully) the people they had encountered. Yes, I am a victim of it, and still learning and growing from these shortcomings along the way. For those who know this kind of writing, or the person I am, I do really need a hug of recognition that I exist. Just be there for me. For those who do not, please know your persons better. For my future partner, I am hoping that you are there for me. It is already difficult to live as a neurodivergent person, what more a struggling ND to be a human person. That's my existential crisis, folks, quarter-life crisis rather. Hopefully we can talk more about finding life while already overcoming the struggle.

i'm genuinely so stupid. i'm bad at everything, from language to math to science to just plain logic and reasoning. i'm very uncreative as well. it takes me a long time to develop most skills and i usually don't reach any level above average even with a lot of effort. i interact with others and can FEEL how they're so much more quick-thinking than me, how profound their ideas are compared to mine, the ease where they connect concepts and come up with solutions... but even without comparing myself with others, i struggle from the stupid mistakes i seem to make 24/7. what's wrong with me? why am i so dumb? how the hell did i even get into [NCR]? i only really succeed in tasks that can be done through PURE effort, like memorizing enough info to not fail some multiple choice quiz. but i'm so, so lacking in natural talent. the only thing i've got going for me is that i'm curious and genuinely want to learn and get better, but pure curiosity can only make one knowledgeable, and i can't seem to find a cure to my inherent lack of fluid intelligence. i don't know, maybe i'm being too pessimistic about all of this. i really don't want this post to discourage anyone who may be struggling from the same thing. i just wanted to get it out there.

[NCR] is not a commuter-friendly campus. If you book a ride-hailing service like Grab, inDrive, [REDACTED_NAME], or JoyRide, drivers will often avoid accepting the booking. And if they do accept it, they sometimes make you feel guilty for choosing [NCR] as your pickup point because they know how long it can take to exit the campus. Once inside any of the gates, drivers are required to surrender their ID at a designated ID claim area, which is usually located farther inside. During rush hours, internal traffic becomes so heavy that it can take 30 to 45 minutes just for your ride to reach you, or to get out of campus. For commuters and drivers, that’s a significant waste of time and money. These are not small inconveniences; they directly affect students, faculty, staff, and visitors who rely on public and ride-hailing transport. [NCR] seriously needs to address these systemic issues. At the moment, it feels like the campus has resigned itself to being a car-centric institution. Please do better, [NCR]. If the university truly upholds its mission of forming “men and women for others,” then creating a more commuter-friendly, accessible campus should be part of that commitment.

Do you feel like something is missing? like a part of you is empty? Not many want to hear it, but the answer is God. of all things changing is just. too much for me (and i'd truly rather not find out na it was just me who had such big feelings .. and who grew to have such big feelings over the past year and a half.. There's an obvious answer to this question, but I still have to ask. Is there a way to make a complaint about a professor and make the punishment actually stick? Ideally, the answer is yes, but several precedents have been set, that show the answer is a big no. Specially with this particular prof. I graduated here years ago, and I've never had him as a prof, but already heard about him. Strict prof, no nonsense, has a tendency of humiliating students a la [REDACTED_NAME], and has a particularly sick fascination with female students and particularly chinese ones, like my baby sister. I looked into him a bit. Over the years, particularly in the pre-pandemic era, several complaints have been filed against him in conjunction with his tendency to leer at female students and making them uncomfortable. Heck, the most striking one, for me, was that incident of him "coming across" a chinese girl whose gender preference is the same sex as her, and telling her "you could do better than that." Better than that? She's damn well free to pursue her own desires for companionship without the prejudice of others, most especially her prof. But wait, there's more. He was known to give out comments about her outfit choices, proactively offering to further tutor the poor girl to improve her grades. and looking her way more than others in most classes. And there's the way he looks at them. Not just this girl, but my sister and all the others he has his eye on. My sis can't describe it well, but she did liken it to a lion staring at its prey. Uncomfortable, invasive, wild. Appreciative, though in a very leery way. She won't elaborate on it, refused to elaborate on it, but she's afraid. She's afraid, and I can't do a thing about it. It's hard. Yes, I can make a complaint, and even if a punishment is meted out, it's very light when it comes to him. An oh-so valuable professor, the best in his field in the whole country. Irreplaceable, they call him. Multiple complaints that pushed through, and through it all, he remains the same, his nature remains the same, unchanged and unpunished all because his expertise is unique. I wonder, though, if his family endures these gazes of his, his methods, his perversity, his maliciousness. I hear they're living peacefully in Pampanga. I so do hope his behavior improves. And that my sister's uni experience remains some of the most wonderful experiences she'll ever have.

Reality check. Accept failure and stop acting like you're owed everything. Yes, call out injustice, but not everything that doesn't work out for you is one. You can throw everything you have. Give your all. You can work all night, make everyone your enemy, skip your meals, lose sleep, use all your money, run until you drop. Blood, sweat, tears, mind, and spirit. All of it. In the end, you still might not get what you want, what you've been fighting for. Grades, love, success, forgiveness, happiness, friendship, a way home. It'll suck, but it will happen. Deal with it. The universe doesn't owe it to you. Cry like a baby. Crash out. Whatever. Life's unfair. But what will you do about it? For real? Just keep going. Stop the blame game or crying foul when the world doesn't work fairly anyway.

I feel so bad whenever I feel this way because I know how privileged I am. I know that our family is comfortable enough to send me to this school while still being able to afford food on the table. But the thing is, I feel this way because of them. The pressure to perform well to fulfill their dreams for me. Hell, even my course was chosen by them. Every day, I feel less and less like I have control over myself, my actions. My true passion is nowhere close to what I’m doing now, so I just end up feeling exhausted whenever I finish any class. I can’t do this anymore, but when I express this to them, they tell me I’m being dramatic. They say that my problems aren’t that serious yet because I’m still young. LOL, I might as well just disappear!

Tutorial classes should definitely be considered as regular classes that count towards the minimum units requirement for full-time faculty. Who’s to say professors work less for a class of just 11 or less people? The prof prepares a syllabus. The prof has a lesson plan. The prof shows up to class, commutes or drives to work, and is dressed appropriately. The prof has to check papers. The prof issues assignments. In a smaller class, in fact, the prof has to be MORE present to those small number of students because there is more room for real academic conversation to happen. Is the [REDACTED_NAME] admin’s definition of “work” equal to “the more papers a prof has to check, the more that prof is worth it and gets paid more”? That’s a sick way of thinking, if that’s the case. It’s not a human-centered way of thinking. And that ought to change. Perhaps, disincentivizing tutorial classes may even lead to the opposite effect: profs become more lax and less caring because they’re only paid per student who enlists?

I used to think that an ME degree was a guaranteed ticket to a big starting salary. I was wrong. Despite graduating with honors, I’ve spent the last seven months feeling like I’m being ghosted by the companies I'm applying to. While some local and international employers still have a soft spot for the "ME" brand, the reality is that the job market is a different beast than it was a decade or two ago. Many MNCs now couldn’t care less about your specific degree. They care more about your skills and how well you can back up your experience under pressure. Even a Magna Cum Laude honor in itself might not be the ‘silver bullet’ it once was. If I were to start over, and I wish I could, I would seek out leadership and head roles in orgs. I would also prioritize stacking at least two internships and make an actual impact during my time there. In applying to companies and answering interview questions, I really noticed that a lot of companies prioritize what you can actually do over the sole pedigree of your degree program. I see my non-[NCR] and ME friends who prioritized leadership and internships landing high-paying roles early, while I’m still searching. Well, it is not surprising that the Philippines became a center of exorcism in the Asia with its non-DOH licensed Saint [REDACTED_NAME] Center of Spiritual Liberation on Exorcism. Filipinos would rather believe that their aunt or daughter is fighting a spiritual battle than schizophrenia. You can watch these videos in Youtube by ABS-CBN or [REDACTED_NAME] Soho documenting these stories by priests who say that they are fighting a spiritual battle against literal demons. It is , but the Philippines is literally talking about demons in the comment section rather than a mental health crisis caused by poverty or economic realities. The Church does not have a good history with possessions either. It had scandal before in 1970s Germany with [REDACTED_NAME] with her parents and the exorcist being charged with neglected homicide. Yea sure, the Church claims to have reformed itself by doing psychological screening and have mental health consultants who are also devout Catholics. We totally can trust the Church for accountability. The cold hard truth is that the Church is promoting an unethical medical malpractice. By definition, exorcism is a treatment that frees a person from "demonic entities". No peer-reviewed medical paper has proven that any of these exorcisms are more than the placebo effect in action. Suspiciously, those documented cases seem to be psychiatric or psychological conditions. The Church is not to blame because the country barely has medical institutions and trained mental health professionals to handle our mental health care. Indeed, they are even giving an alternative solution by expanding their trained "exorcists". We should be ashamed of this reality because the common Filipino would rather go to a free priest than a P4000 consultation with a psychologist. It's not only about the Church, but the mindset of the people. The Church maintains a hard belief in demons. The truth is that the Church admits only a negligible are actually inflicted with demonic obsession or possession (I would say virtually zero). The fact that families are willing to take their loved ones to the Church for exorcism is already a negative mindset for the victims of mental health when the most probably scientific explanations are more appropriate. I am no psychologist nor lawyer, but something has to be done. We need to regulate these exorcisms, because no Catholic hospital runs on just miracles, but these "exorcists" claim to have a special powers that heal people mentally. What sane society will allow people to hope and pray for miracles to cure tuberculosis when we have medicine and scientific treatments? This is not offending religious feelings, but a public health crisis. The Church is stepping into public health and promoting quackery. This is morally and ethically disgusting especially when majority of people looking for exorcisms are the poor. The politicians just ignore it because the Church is a powerful entity. Also, the government admits their failure by relying on these institutions.

Stories of an alum. I had a psych prof (I know you psych profs are reading this!) back in the 2010s for a class called “Gen Psych”. It was a required class under [REDACTED_NAME]’s old curriculum. Not sure what it is now under [NCR]’s new curriculum. Anyways, i loved this prof and her personality. But I hated the class so much. A class like General Psychology could have been made SO interesting, but the way this class was taught to me was so memorization-based. Every exam that we had was just a test of we memorized the things we learned. To be frank, I don’t remember any of the lessons we had. Why? Because HOW we were assessed was grounded on the fact that students had to memorize stuff — the pressure. All tests. No grounding in real life. No talk about mental health. Something like THAT could have made psych interesting to non-psych majors. By contrast, I had the great Doctor [REDACTED_NAME] Angeles for a philo class. THAT class I still remember. What I learned I still remember. Doc A grounded everything in real life. No theories just floating in the air. Everything practically useful. During my time at least, she did not have LTs. There was one oral exam for her, but the principles of her ethical decision framework still stick with me today. LEARNING SHOULD NOT BE A NUMBERS GAME. (@[NCR] admin, listen [REDACTED_NAME] please.) Your students might ACE their long tests, they might even spend all night studying for it. Long test marks generate hard numbers, which of course the admin likes. But true and genuine learning comes if what is being learned is integrated into real life. And memorizing facts for a long test, at least from my experience, can never achieve that. We will all forget it. Cheers to professors like Doc A, whose lessons, even after YEARS of taking her class, I still remember fondly. [NCR] admin, please think of the long-term. Numbers is fast data. Encourage your faculty to not just focus on generating numbers for you, but to generate real learning, grounded in [NCR] values and the Catholic faith, that students can take for the rest of their life.

How should Taiwan settle the political dispute with China? 👍 join China as a Special Administrative Region (SAR) just like Hong Kong and Macau ❤️ declare independence as the Republic of Taiwan instead of Republic of China to be recognized as separate and distinct from China 😮 integrate the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) into the democratic multi-party system of the Republic of China and takeover Mainland China as the ROC

Taiwan isn’t part of the UN because of the One China Policy of the People’s Republic of China, which claims Taiwan to be part of it. Why not rename Taiwan from "Republic of China" to "Republic of Taiwan"? They could join international organizations like the UN as "Taiwan" rather than "Chinese Taipei" and as a member rather than an observer. That way, they could be considered an independent country distinct from Mainland China rather than claiming to be another "China". People in Taiwan nowadays prefer to identify as "Taiwanese" rather than "Chinese" and their passport cover even says "Taiwan".

Long winded rant ahead. I feel like this isn't an opinion held by most ND's but I've always appreciated when someone directly tells me I'm being idiosyncratic and in which manner. So, unbeknownst to them, I can better adjust how I can mask in the social environment. It rarely happens but when it does it clears the air so much better than having to tiptoe around social dynamics I'm completely and wholeheartedly foreign to even if it makes me look really dumb in retrospect. Masking, to me, for what my opinion's worth, is part of the game we have to play whether or not we're ND. Everyone adjusts their behaviors and mannerisms in subtle ways depending on the environment. It's just that, as a ND, its slippage and cracks are usually significantly more disruptive to the social order of the moment. Its more exhausting for ND's to adjust and conform to a society that was never designed for them in mind and I don't think that it should be that way but in the meantime, it is.

Hot take: If a professor cannot lecture properly and convey his or her message efficiently and smoothly, the professor should NOT be allowed to teach college students. Period. Perhaps it could still work for master’s or doctoral students, where learning is way more independent in nature. But most college students won’t go that path. And college students DESERVE a lecturer who will impart to them what they need for their future careers, not someone who just sticks to theories that will ultimately be forgotten. If you cannot lecture properly and effectively, stick to something like research or policy making.

hello! i'm a freshie, and honestly, i'm not completely sure about my course. during the [REDACTED_DEMONYM], I wasn’t able to include the course I really wanted in my choices. right now, my program is in SOSS. during the break last year, though, I kept thinking that I might actually want to be in SOSE, because i realized i'm more inclined toward science and math. but at the same time, i also like my course now. it’s broad, flexible, useful, and practical in many ways. on top of that, shifting would also mean additional expenses for my family. the thing is, i don’t dislike my current course; i just don’t appreciate it the way i appreciate the course i originally wanted. because of that, my path feels really blurry right now. i'm not sure what to pursue anymore, and i’m starting to have second thoughts. part of me also wonders if i should simply give this course a real shot. maybe, over time, i’ll learn to appreciate it the same way i appreciate the course i originally wanted. after all, i came from a STEM background, so it might just be natural for me right now to lean more toward science and math. in reality, though, i sometimes think my current course might actually be better in terms of the job opportunities i could get in the future compared to the course I’m aiming for. so yeah—it’s honestly confusing. what do you think i should consider in this situation? what factors should matter more, and what shouldn’t?

School departments and home orgs’ (and my friends’ and my) unpopular opinions: MEA - Week of tambay? Check. Alumni relations and career opportunities? Secured. Support system? Stronger than your Wi-Fi signal. And the pogis? Oh, they’ll definitely help you survive this hellhole of a program. PSYCHE - Always has an event, like there’s no tomorrow. The faculty’s goated, the org’s even more goated, and best part? The school’s practically guaranteeing you’ll be a board passer. Most improved faculty and org this school year. Though, fair warning: the girls here might just make you question your sexuality. ACTM - Active, fun, and full of outings—basically a party department. Marketing’s top tier, public speaking’s a vibe, and we got the cutest profs and students (seriously, it’s like a beauty contest). Best org in [REDACTED_NAME] this year? I’d say so. BOX - Advocacy events and bonuses? Double check. A home away from home, with the best department in SOSE and holistic premed vibes. Lovebox? More like love everything. The events are 10/10. MISA and COMPSAT - Career development, [REDACTED_NAME] energy, and backed by P&G. Hackathons so cool you might actually learn something. But hey, can someone please fix [REDACTED_NAME]? And maybe take a shower while you’re at it? ACHES - The nerds who are secretly hilarious. Chemistry in [REDACTED_NAME]’s criminally underrated. It’s super progressive and innovative, and the lab’s a second home. Honestly, one of the best programs if you want a double degree. This is international thesis-level stuff. HSS - Smart, but intimidating as heck. They’re going to be our doctors, but wait... where are those ASMPh visual-looking undergrads? Active in ASMPh? Yes. Worthy connections? Definitely. Want to go to med school? Go here, and you’ll be that stellar public health advocate.

I know a lot of people complain about people who focus too much on org work and end up neglecting academic work, especially group requirements, but can we also talk about people who willingly commit to org responsibilities and then suddenly ghost midway because they realize they "can't handle" the load? There's absolutely nothing wrong with choosing to prioritize your studies. That's why we're called "student leaders" because being a student will always come first. It's just incredibly difficult because they never think about the people who stay and try to keep things running. After all, who else would carry the weight they decided to offload without any explanation at all? It's incredibly inconsiderate. The least you can do is communicate your situation so that everyone can adjust and redistribute the workload.

After knowing that my prof in my major graduated [REDACTED_NAME] in UP but isn’t really that highly proficient when it comes to teaching (has a low mean test score for all of their sections, answers questions with some sense of ambiguity or uncertainty, and discussions aren’t really that extensive compared to when I read our book, search for the topics in the internet, or ask friends from different sections who have a different prof), I gradually realized that graduating with honors does not really reflect how well-rounded you are as a person. Some cannot unfortunately translate their intelligence or college credentials into practice. I know this is probably a far stretch judgement against my prof, but most of the time, when I learn somebody graduated as a cum laude, I usually get amazed. But when the prof casually told our class that they’re a [REDACTED_NAME] grad, I only felt nothing; devoid from any amazement. Cause idk, if I was a teacher and saw that the mean score in all of my classes were extravagantly low, I would question my competencies. As compared to my prof in a core subject who failed a Big4 entrance exam (but still got in through a talent test?), that prof was probably one of the best teachers I ever had; taught us beyond theories and books. Showed us empathy and social sensitivity. And you wouldn’t question that this prof failed that entrance test because the prof is highly articulate and covers our topics at ease. I guess some people have the gift and character to teach, while others don’t.

In a highly globalized world, characterized by travel and the mingling of many cultures, it actually makes sense if a number of Masses and liturgies be offered in Latin. This isn’t about being stuck in some glorious past, but it’s about practicality. Latin is still the official language of the Church. If we knew some basic Latin, and if places around the world offered some [REDACTED_NAME] in Latin (not all of course), we can recite and sing basic prayers like the Our Father and the Salve [REDACTED_NAME] no matter where we are in the world, be it Manila, Beijing, Barcelona, Pretoria, Washington DC, Buenos Aires, or the Vatican. Many Latin-English missals exist, and resources online are plentiful. Plus, Mass in Latin is just plain cool, too.

I have been with my girlfriend for three and a half years. We are still together, but it has become very hard. She still will not introduce me to her parents, and over time that has started to weigh on me. We are students, so I am not asking for a ring or for marriage. I am asking to be acknowledged. I want to feel that I am a real part of her life and not someone kept on the side. I respected her pace and told myself that she might need more time, but after three and a half years it becomes difficult not to question where I stand. When you care about someone, you include them in your life. You do not hide them or delay their place forever. Being kept separate for this long makes you feel temporary, even when the relationship is serious. We have not broken up, but loving someone while constantly doubting your place is exhausting. At some point, you have to be honest with yourself and ask whether you are being patient or simply stuck. Waiting without clarity slowly turns into pain, and that is where I am now.

Ultimately, my goal is to communicate more fluently and minimize reliance on code-switching, scripts, and the like.

Amid the ongoing corruption scandals gripping the country, anger and passion is increasing among the public. Truly this is an opportunity for true change, but can also be a chance of others waiting in line for their turn. With that said, I am very concerned over what was once a meme page on colleges. International State [DEPARTMENT]ould just post funny facts and grievances on how universities operated, and at times there would be occasional jabs against government. However, it has been increasingly similar to Duterte - campaign content, mainly pointing at a single source of the country's problems. I was once a supporter of those messages, hence I am hearing rhymes in its posts. One of their recent posts was connecting the constitution to legalizing the system of corruption. Unfortunately, it is gaining traction and its is difficult to be a dissenting voice in a sea of yes men and angry netizens. I hope this post gives wisdom to maintain being critical and vigilant against such false prophets in these exciting times. I have unfollowed them and I hope you do the same before they start spouting generalizations favoring certain politicians.

I think I’ve reached a point in my college life where I want to start quiet-quitting. I don’t plan on flunking my classes, or being pabigat sa group works. But, I’m not aiming to reach for the stars everytime either. I get that for some it can come across as lazy or privileged. But is it really wrong if I’m just not willing to sacrifice so much of myself to achieve accolades and awards? I want to preserve my mental and physical health. I want to have time for my friends and have a social life. I want to be able to explore my hobbies and personal interests. I just want to be a happy and healthy human being. Mediocrity doesn’t received recognition, but I’d rather be average and happy, than be exceptional and miserable.

I’m tired of not being able to make myself a priority even when I want to. I’m tired of devoting so much time and energy to people I fall for but turn out to not be the right person for me. It’s not their fault; it’s mine. I choose to fall for them even if they don’t return the same energy I give them. I’m tired of constantly wanting to be loved by others when I can’t even love myself. I do everything I can to show my peers that I appreciate their company. I show up to parties, events, org meetings, and whenever I am needed or wanted, even when I badly need time for and only for myself. I’m tired of feeling like everyone loves everyone but me. I see my peers and everyone getting along and enjoying each others’ company while I’m just an extra guy sitting with them on the same table but not seen or wanted to be spoken to. I see everyone’s phones full of casual messages, while my phone has none or just has work-related messages. I always see my peers with their friends while I’m just alone by myself. I’m tired of calling someone my friend when they are just an acquaintance in reality. The only reason we talk and see each other is when we have meetings or have something related to work. But what do we do regardless of that? Nothing. For once, I want to know what it’s like to have REAL friends. I’m tired. I’m tired. I’m tired.

Dear You, How have your classes been treating you thus far in the semester? I'm writing to check up on you - I hope you don't mind. I also hope you're surviving your Philosophy readings, Math assignments, Science lessons or just that one subject that always feels heavier than it should. I haven't the slightest idea if you've been tired lately, excited, or just quietly counting down the days to the weekend. But I do so hope you've still found the time to breathe between it all. On slower days, I wonder if you'd want to play video games with me - Cyberpunk , [REDACTED_NAME], Halo, GTA, FNaF, Minecraft, TLoU, Half-Life 3, [REDACTED_NAME], among others - if you're into these games as much as I am. The kinds of games where we don't rush to win, just talk while the hours pass as it rains outside while we stay warm and cozy inside. Or maybe you're more into watching cartoons, movies late at night? The Owl House, Adventure Time, [REDACTED_NAME]. I could be Jinu and you - my [REDACTED_NAME]. Just, you know, the comforting kinds that make life feel less loud for a while. I'd be happy with that too. I want to listen to some music with you too. Me? I'm fond of songs from the 80s - it's a childhood thing, from my dad. Specifically those by a little known band called Fra Lippo [REDACTED_NAME] whose songs I am enamored of insofar as the undergraduate thesis I'm writing is an analysis of their songs. But any genre will do fine. Or just walk around campus with me? Reminds me a whole lot of home down south where you can feel the sun shining on your face, the wind in your long (or short) hair. Home where the air stinks not of fumes, that has lush greenery everywhere and where land and see are locked in a tight embrace. But mostly, I just hope you're okay. And if this sounds like I already know you, it's because I'm writing to her - the one I haven't met yet. I don't know where you are on campus, what course you're taking, or if we've already passed each other without realizing it. I just know you're out there somewhere, living your own life. I'm still looking for you. No rush. Just hoping that, one day, we'll find each other. Sincerely, The one who really wants to meet you

The amount of AI usage I see is absolutely sickening and it drives me crazy. I've only ever used chatgpt once because I really had no other choice, and it made me feel so awful I nearly cried. Call me overdramatic but the environment shouldn't be destroyed because you need to summarize notes. I'm a 3rd year. We grew up writing essays by hand. What happened to our generation. No, I'm not going to "get with the times" if said times are wasting and destroying our world. It makes me ill whenever there's a class presentation and AI is used for say a product example. Photoshop exists. It'd be weird if you didn't have an artist friend. Sure handcrafted might look "rougher" but at least there's real, handcrafted effort in a crappy hodgepodge of images as opposed to something thrown together by an environment-destroying machine. The absolute worst instance I've seen AI used was when someone brought up chatgpt to help introduce themselves on day 1 of class. I'm sorry. WHAT?! Am I going insane? Am I the only person to think it's insane to use chatgpt to help introduce yourself?! Raise. Your. Standards. Pick up a pencil. Write that 300 word essay yourself because trust that I will judge you if you pull up chatgpt for the easiest things ever. I'm proud I'm not an AI user. And I sincerely hope I'm not insane for thinking such stuff.

Stuff that is supposed to be public knowledge that [NCR] students need to know. For a university that espouses “liberal education”, [NCR] is rigid and backward when it comes to curriculum. “Faculty are REQUIRED to use the approved official CLOs when the course was designed and approved” —>—> Good intention. Uniformity and consistency and quality of education is needed. However, this does not take into account the BIG fact that the world is changing. Student needs are changing. Student desires are changing. To stick to old CLOs that don’t address the context of the now is backward thinking. And in order for the faculty to change the CLOs, he or she still has to go through a process. Admin [REDACTED_NAME]! “You are REQUIRED to do 45 hours of onsite hours” —>—> Again, good intention. Quality education is needed, and human interaction is a must. Advanced math calculus classes, for example, would probably want to meet as many times as possible because you’ll want the teacher to be there for the students. But not all classes are math classes. Not all classes are physics problem-solving classes that need the clear demonstration of the teacher. Some classes are READING-HEAVY. A classics or literature class. You CANNOT rush that. Why? Because in reading classics and novels, you need time to PONDER and THINK. You CANNOT rush reading a classic and expect the student to take it all in, just to fulfill some required minimum hours to meet onsite. If there is anything that the pandemic has taught us, it is that async CAN be done. And it can be blended with onsite for maximum benefit. Admin, have we not learned to be more flexible? “It would be nice if [NCR] rises in the global university rankings!" But then, also: "We want to save money on faculty salary by disincentivizing small classes of less than 12 students!” —>—> Dear [NCR] students, you must know something revolutionary (dare I say). Those classes of 30 students that you have? Sure, that’s the norm. But it does NOT have to be that way all the time. There exist classes of less than 12 students, and those are called “tutorial classes”. Unfortunately, tutorial classes are NOT counted in the required minimum load of full-time faculty members, and so many of those smaller classes you want to join don’t exist or eventually get dissolved. In other words, besides those tutorial classes, your full-time faculty are required to still teach 30 units every single school year. No exceptions. Students, here’s one more thing you need to know. When your professors DO choose to have those small “tutorial classes” of less than 12 students, they literally only get paid based on how many students join. If 11 students join, swerte ka. If only 1 student joins, sorry ka. And yes, no matter how many students join, 1 or 30, [REDACTED_NAME]’s admin on paper STILL requires the same type of preparation and dedication as that of a normal class of 30 students. EVEN IF the professor only gets paid for that 1 student. So yes, students, you can theoretically have small literature English classes of just 10 students dedicated to analyzing only [REDACTED_NAME] for the entire sem. Or perhaps a bio class solely dedicated to the study of the cardiovascular system with just 5 students who aspire to be heart surgeons — all of that is possible!!! But the [NCR] admin, through its rigid structural policies, limits that creativity and disincentivizes it. Ironically, and I know [NCR] admin knows this, one of the criteria for how high or low a university ranks in global rankings is that of faculty-student ratio. In other words, the less faculty-student ratio the classes of a university has, the higher the chance at global university rankings. Unless... The admin doesn’t really want [NCR] to fair in the global rankings? Admin, what do you have to say about all this? Are you part of the problem, or can you be the light to the solution/s? Dare I remind everyone, this is besides ALL the other problems [NCR] faces, such as flooding during heavy rains. [FW admin please edit this out if not appropriate] Funny thing is, everyone knows who you guys are, [NCR] admin. Your names are in our circulars and websites. And yet it is your name that loses their public credibility the more you are part of the problem and not the solution.

Pov: It’s and the second semester of your third year in [NCR] is about to start. You just got back from a trip abroad where constant reminders of what you two were mocked you as you walked the streets of an unfamiliar environment. Merchandise from shows they liked. Their favorite flowers that you got them for your anniversary. Songs that you two used to listen to together being projected from the shops along the road. Their favorite milk tea shop that you didn’t even know was available abroad. Everything the universe can do to keep you from forgetting. You land back in NAIA with no “glad you’re back safe baby” because now it seems like they could care less about you. And whether that really is the case will never be known because they never gave the chance for proper closure or even a conversation about why things ended. There was also no conversation about how you two could move forward apart from one another. The trip abroad was enough of a distraction to keep you from checking what socials you aren’t already blocked on though. You open spotify and check their profile out of curiosity, only to find out that they unfollowed you. Actually, that’s incorrect. You’re outright blocked on spotify which you didn’t even know was possible. And now more than ever you’re confused as to why they so desparately want to wash their hands of you. Was it really just because they were growing tired of the relationship? Did you not give enough? You wont get the chance to find out anymore. Despite what frustration you may feel over the circumstances, you still cant bring yourself to feel anger towards them. You still remember them as someone who was loving and kind. You still see them as someone fun and gentle. All you can do now slowly but surely move on.
