tw: sa when u lowkey don’t have anyone to talk to and it’s 2:16 am on dec 29, so u end up typing ur feelings on the [NCR] fw 🙁 admin pls post this because i want advice. not just "it gets better" comments. i want advice to move on from this. and it’s kinda obvious it’s me if u know me, so i gotta keep this as vague as possible. idk. maybe this is a cry for help. but fuck, i hate this. knowing a lot of this is my fault. knowing i’m lonely because of me. because i self-sabotage. i got sa’d by someone in my friend group, and for months i genuinely thought i was a cheater. it took therapy and a few friends for it to finally click that no, i was sa’d. i was uncomfortable in that friend group but i stayed because it was what i had. until i self-sabotaged. for context: i’m really bad at social cues and volume control, and i mirror people’s humor to fit in (which is on me, especially since there was a lot of casual ableism and racism). i don’t set boundaries because i’m scared of being alone, so they’d make comments about how flat and crippled i am. which btw, cripple is a slur, but yeah. i let them walk all over me because i don’t know how to talk. then i made an insensitive joke trying to fit in and i fucked up. no denying that. i said a slur. even if i can reclaim it, it was still harmful. other people were saying it so i thought maybe i could too? pare-parehas naman yung humor. still, that’s on me. what they did was wrong, but so was i. a wrong doesn’t cancel out another wrong. i left the friend group. yeah, i was ashamed, but it was also the perfect way out. i didn’t want to be ridiculed for my body, i didn’t want to feel inferior over things i can’t control, and i didn’t want to be around my assaulter. what’s worse is that i miss them. my assaulter included. or maybe i just miss the idea of them. the idea of having friends. because icl, i’m lonely as fuck. can't hate on them too because they don't know i was assaulted so, and yeah, that’s also on me. i don’t talk unless spoken to. i leave as soon as class ends. i don’t reach out. i stay in my dorm doomscrolling. i have online friends and i love them, but they’re continents away, and i don’t open up to them the way they open up to me. when i talk about my shit, they get shocked. when they vent, it’s the most first-world problem ever. “my mom wants me to go to the store at 8am” “i’m getting cancelled on [REDACTED_NAME] for liking this character” meanwhile i’m trying to get my left leg to un-numb itself because i slept wrong and fucked up my back, and now i’m gonna be late for school. yeah, i should get that checked, but when i went back to my hometown, i realized how many friends i have there. i’m not famous or anything, but compared to manila? i actually have support systems. i don’t need to mirror. they don’t call me slurs. the only issue is my family. i tried opening up about my sa and all i got was victim-blaming. “you shouldn’t have trusted him.” “you should’ve kept your legs closed.” i asked if i could loa to get my shit together and now my dad won’t even talk to me. like girl?? one more day of this and istg i’ll tell mama you never quit your addiction and we’ll see how you like being blamed. all i do to cope is make art, tweet, consume media, doomscroll. sometimes even goon because i’m so numb and i just want to feel something, even if it’s only for half an hour. then i cry or dissociate in the bathroom while my roommate probably wonders why i’ve been in there so long with no water running. manifesting i get adopted by a friend group or something because i’m exhausted. i just want to be loved in manila. and i know i am. by two people. my bf, who’s genuinely the sweetest and most patient person i know. no matter how chaotic he gets, he’s my person. but he has his own life, and i want him to live it. i don’t want to depend on him. i don’t want to be the girl whose only friend is her boyfriend. and my hb. our schedules never align and it fucks me up. he lives too far, so we can’t even hang out. i miss having a friend group. i want to laag. i want to take pics and stupid tiktoks. i want presentation nights about random shit. i want to go karaoke. i want cinema buddies and yap about it in jollibee. but after being sa’d, i can’t trust people. at the same time, i hate that i still want to be desired in the most disgusting way possible. like i want to be wanted without being touched, seen without being known, consumed without being hurt. i don’t even know if that’s desire or just damage pretending to be one. i can’t bring myself to shower because no matter how hard i scrub, it never feels like enough. my skin doesn’t feel clean, it feels haunted. like my body remembers things my brain is trying to forget. i drink, even alone, just to prove to myself that i still have choice. that if i’m numb or ruined or reckless, it’s because i decided to be. sometimes i feel like my body isn’t mine anymore, just something that got taken and returned broken. i exist in it, but i don’t live in it. i swing between wanting to disappear completely and wanting someone to want me so badly it feels like self-harm. i hate that this happened to me. i hate that it rewired my brain, my trust, my sense of self. i hate that i can name all of this and still feel powerless against it. i’m so fucked bruh. lmao.

I'm already 20 and yet, I'm still struggling with being social. I'm only great at professional networking and academic communication, but absolutely suck at creating and maintaining close relationships. I'm always the quiet friend in the group, the friend who's always listening and reacting but is not sharing stories. I genuinely have no social skills and I want to change this so bad, but I don't know where to start. I've had a lot of issues with most of my school environments, and it fucked me up to the point where I only focused on becoming an academic weapon with big dreams without building actual social skills. I had no friends for years, and I didn't care. I never got to develop things like how to sound more interesting and funny, or how to have a long one-on-one conversation with a friend. My very few interests aren't even that popular, and I'm scared people would think of me as boring for these, so I don't express them at all. I don't go out partying, I don't play video games, I don't watch KDramas, I don't play or watch sports, I don't read [REDACTED_NAME] novels, I can't even play an instrument. I'm just bland. Even if I wanna make friends and be closer to someone, it's hard because most of the time? We don't even like the same stuff, and it's much harder for me to fake it because I don't even know what to say after, and people can really tell if you're faking it. In my friendships, I feel like I'm not contributing that much as I can't even talk about anything in my life. It's either I'm laughing with them, listening to them, reacting with filler words, or replying with basically predictable stuff. In the end, I would see them being way closer together than they are with me, and I would spiral at night thinking it's because my life's too boring and my lack of social skills is making it worse. It feels so bad because I want to feel these very close friendships too. I want my friends to feel more open, comfortable, and expressive with me the same way they are with each other. Studying in one of the [NCR]'s most respected programs with high career prospects means nothing if your social skills suck and the loneliness is eating you alive. I'm desperate, and I want to know if there's still hope for people like me to improve their social skills. What should I do? I just want to change and make the most out of my friendships and potential relationships asap.

i’m a completely different person btw, unrelated to the other posts about this topic—got the gears turning lang. when you say your piece isn’t to excuse freeloaders, it’s a little contradictory alongside language that places yourself among them (e.g. we, i, etc.)? nonetheless, i think it’s important to have the distinctions clear. when we say “freeloaders” those are people that literally have not contributed, and have been given the benefit of the doubt multiple times yet just refuse to help. no explanation and if [REDACTED_NAME], their uncooperative behavior has already affected the group/work. basically, it’s damage done that’s irreparable or mahirap na remedyohan. i think you aren’t the target audience of those posts, unless of course if the shoe fits. yes, [REDACTED_NAME] toxic kind of perfectionists in the group—those na unreasonable, those who have sent such messages and threatened groupmates—but do not lump them with those that initiate group works and have been reasonable and patient with said “freeloaders”. and at the cost pa of the group work and sanity of everyone in the group. i have been on both sides. as someone who initiates group works or leads, have the patience, understanding, and empathy [REDACTED_NAME] and yourself. respect normal working hours, don’t spam on sundays unless urgent, and NEVER act like mas superior ka [REDACTED_NAME]. may recent akong experience wherein someone who slacked for most part of the project suddenly was antsy at the latter parts but was disrespectful when asking for updates, lacked timeliness, and overall, rude. that is not right. anyway, it’s just having basic courtesy, common sense ([REDACTED_NAME]), respect [REDACTED_NAME], and knowing your place din in the group with regard [REDACTED_NAME] to contribution—[REDACTED_NAME], responsibilidad and possibly, utang mo yan sa group. as someone who may not be contributing as much, struggling, burnt out, so on so forth—be transparent with your groupmates—and if hindi [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME], please talk to your professor. [REDACTED_NAME], magcommunicate ka. i’ve had groupmates who were radio silent when pinged during working hours, ghosted, never did their tasks, yet had the audacity to brag the final group grade—at the cost of the sanity and mental health of the other members. overall, i wish we become more critical when it comes to these kinds of issues. [REDACTED_NAME] it’s clear dito that what’s missing is just overall, [REDACTED_NAME] using your position, situation, or reputation. there’s an urgent call to retain self-reflexivity, accountability, and respect to others. let’s not misconstrue realities as an excuse for obligation and accountability to your group.

I think it’s worth pausing and approaching this with a bit more understanding and sensitivity because the OAA team is very kind, hands-on, and just. Our school has systems in place to make sure scholarships are awarded thoughtfully, not randomly. Many scholars carry different situations. Some are merit-based, such as Dirlist, AFMs, and DOST (under their merit scholarship which awards 20k per sem) so these scholars are actually able to afford the usual stuff people do anyway and/or even way more but people just like to assume differently which can be frustrating sometimes because I know my rich scholar friends always say that they have to say they are merit based to not get judged for what they say, do, and post even tho a good number of them have around the same standing as non-scholar ateneans. For the financial based one, others naman work multiple jobs to support themselves, and some are already undergoing or have undergone reviews when their financial circumstances change, which genuinely does happen. I personally know previous financial scholars whose household incomes increased five times and for the blessed ones whose families were presented with good opportunities, even up to fourteen to twenty times compared to the average household income of other scholars combined, and they were indeed reassessed and had their scholarships discontinued. The OAA has been genuinely hands-on and careful in ensuring that support goes to those who truly need it. They have systems where the person in question can actually pass multiples files such as letters, payslips, etc. to really get the whole financial picture. Because of that, it feels important to give everyone the benefit of the doubt rather than making people feel uncomfortable or singled out. Before calling the situation and person unjust, I hope there can be space to ask the person involved or reach out to OAA, so the full story is understood with fairness and compassion. Let's not try to look skeptic of those who have it better. Instead, let's respectfully ask or inquire when the time calls for it. There is nothing inherently wrong with wanting to preserve one’s financial standing in college or aspire to be better off. It’s natural for people to feel protective of what their parents have worked very hard for and to be competitive in an environment like this. However, it's as equally important to realize that majority of our parents actually did work as hard. What matters more is choosing to channel that energy into equipping ourselves to sustain or improve our own position, rather than directing frustration or resentment toward others. Let's remember that a lot can happen under a few months or few years. When people work hard and get what they deserve, let them.

I’m an [NCR] alum with a son from Xavier San [REDACTED_NAME]. This page randomly showed up on my feed, and I’ve been seeing posts calling XS boys 🚩🚩🚩 Not gonna lie—it makes me sad that they have this rep. I’m not here to excuse anything, but maybe I can give some context on why they’re like this. 1. They are rich rich. And I don’t mean “comfortable.” I mean unreal levels of money. Like ₱5k baon, giving ₱1k angpaos to classmates during Christmas, type of rich. A lot of them already know they won’t need to job-hunt after graduation because there’s a family business waiting. Still, they’re expected to get good grades and go to good schools. So academics become very “work smarter, not harder.” School is basically a rite of passage. They’re set—and they know it. 2. Very cliquish, but it’s kinda structural. XS San [REDACTED_NAME] is a small school—around nine sections per grade, about 30 students each. Most of these boys have been classmates since kinder, so by high school, everyone already knows everyone. Plus, there’s the Xavier China Experience—two months in China, no parents, foreign country. That kind of shared trauma/bonding really locks friendships in, even without the Fil-Chi angle. 3. [REDACTED_NAME] girl crazy, highkey awkward. They might act nonchalant, but a lot of them genuinely don’t know how to talk to girls. Before college, the only girls they really know are their classmates’ sisters. That’s the all-boys-school life. Medyo kawawa, but real. 4. Certified mama’s boys. Sorry, but yes. I was honestly culture-shocked by how involved parents are. Parents show up to school fairs, are super hands-on, super present. I graduated high school without my parents attending a single PTC—so yeah, XS parents are definitely encouraged to hover. Because of that, a lot of the boys rely heavily on their parents and families. Anyway, hope this helps you navigate dating a [REDACTED_NAME] boy. Good luck!

Schools obsess over teaching the history of civilizations and nations, yet barely touch the most important history of all: the one that raised us. If you grew up in a fatherless home, you should be taught the psychological patterns that often follow—and how they quietly shape your relationships, confidence, and decisions. If you were raised in a household driven by money, survival, or status, you should be taught how that conditions your self-worth and sense of safety. If you were raised in a stable, loving family, you should be taught the invisible advantages you inherited—and the responsibility that comes with them. This isn’t therapy. It’s basic literacy about the human condition. So why is this almost completely absent from the core curriculum? Is it because people who don’t understand their own conditioning are easier to shame, easier to sell to, and easier to control? An educated mind knows history. A free mind knows itself. Do better.

This is OP of If I may add to my prev post, it left a bad taste in my mouth that we discussed the need for more conversations without judgment in the same session — and already may biases na from the start about activists. Love your neighbor as yourself – unless you're on the red tibak side of things, then dni, apparently! I will not name the class where it happened, and I shared my criticism to my prof in the evals (none of this is his fault either, and he's a great prof, too). It's crazy how quick people are to cite the Church's role in organizing the EDSA revolt. How come we can't pull that off again for the anti-corruption rally just last November 30, ? Rhetorical question yan; it's because it's one thing to answer WHY we should fight corruption, but when it comes down to answering HOW we should fight corruption, that's where the solidarity dies. I understand why the two factions are not friends, and that's a conversation not for [NCR] FW lol. For the sake of sticking to a faith-based angle, I'm critical of the fact that we continue to fail to bother to love. We continue to categorize people as Deserving or Undeserving, and it shows. Nothing about all this was generated by ChatGPT or whatever AI tool. It's not even intended to be rage bait. I spoke up as a student genuinely concerned that we were stereotyping activists in a bad light. But all of you in the comments chose not to believe me. Then again, as a woman and a lesbian, I am an Undeserving times two in a religion that empowers toxic masculinity and actively makes Undeservings like me never feel safe. I hope my parents and teachers will forgive me for quitting the Catholic faith. My theology classes were healing the wounds of rejection, but the patronizing, mean-spirited backlash from fellow Catholics (most of them men, some of them lay ministers 🙊) dealt the final blow. I refuse to stay where I will never be accepted. Unfortunately, it just so happened to be God's house.

Response to - TL;DR: Group work fails when expectations are not set early. Posting here won't solve that either. I get how constant pinging and pressure can feel inconsiderate. But posting this on FW won't really help either. In any group work, expectations matter: pacing, roles, timelines, and communication norms should be set early. If those weren't clarified, tension is kind of inevitable. It's also fair to ask: have you shown enough progress or communicated clearly enough to assure your group that you're on track, even if you work differently? Different workloads and rhythms exist, yes—but so does shared accountability. If someone's overstepping, that's best addressed directly. Anonymous call-outs won't build trust, and neither will assuming bad intent.

They say that when you die, your life flashes before your eyes. What they don’t tell you is that sometimes you survive, but the best parts of you don’t come back. I’ve asked myself this question more times than I can count, usually in my quiet moments when there’s no one left to distract me from it: How do you wake up one morning and realize you no longer love someone? If I had known the kind of tragedies our relationship would bring, maybe I would have chosen differently. Maybe I would have protected the five-year friendship that came before us instead of risking it for the opportunity that we could be more. Perhaps, I was also wrong to assume that God intended us to walk the rest of our lives side by side. As our high school friends drifted into their separate college lives, we stuck together. [NCR] was our turf, no matter how repetitive the places we went to. But we are no longer in [REDACTED_NAME] and [NCR]. We will never be those high school kids again. And now that you’re gone, Katip no longer feels like home. The days I have lived without you have far outnumbered the days spent in your presence. Sometimes, I still find myself scanning every corner of the place that raised us in hopes you would appear. I will miss watching you play the sport that raised you. I will miss rolling down the school bus window for a few minutes, just long enough to see you on the field. That small tradition followed us into college... except I only watch you now from afar. I know I was never the kind of girl a life like yours might have demanded. I wasn’t the party girl, wasn’t effortless, wasn’t loud in the ways people notice. Yet you chose to share a quieter life with me, even leaving behind a friend group that only remembered you when it was convenient. That choice meant everything to me. Losing it still does. Why, then, did that love of yours change overnight? I hope that life-changing decision brought you peace. I hope she experiences the love we once deserved. But most of all, I hope you live happily— and that I never hear a word of it.

Dear [REDACTED_DEMONYM] Bucky Stan, My friends have been following you on [REDACTED_NAME] and youve become an inside joke in our friend group. So we made this to make up for the fact that [REDACTED_NAME] is coming back for doomsday: Bucky didn’t expect to disappear halfway across the world (hiding from [REDACTED_NAME] and crying over his breakup with [REDACTED_NAME]) and end up on a campus in [NCR]. He was supposed to stay invisible. Heal. Be alone. Instead, he ran straight into [REDACTED_DEMONYM] Bucky Stan outside MVP—backpack slung over one shoulder, phone in hand, brain clearly full of [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME] almost spilled. “Oh—sorry—WAIT.” Bucky braced himself. “You look like—” [REDACTED_NAME] squinted. “...[REDACTED_NAME] from the Captain America Franchise?!" Silence. Instead of panic or yelling, [REDACTED_NAME] just nodded. “Okay. Cool. I promise I won’t scream. Do you wanna sit?” That was the moment Bucky knew this place—and this person—was different. Days turned into weeks. [REDACTED_NAME] never treated him like a weapon or a myth. Just a guy learning how to exist again. They talked about history, accountability, guilt, and whether people can actually choose who they become. [REDACTED_DEMONYM] [REDACTED_NAME] defended him like it was a recitation grade on the line. Somewhere between the trike rides and quiet sunsets, Bucky fell. Hard. No armor. No biracial vibranium arm. No escape plan. Then—because the universe is never normal—time broke. A familiar shield clattered onto the ground behind them. “Miss me?” [REDACTED_NAME] said, older and younger all at once, like he’d appear out of thin air, with time still clinging to him. Bucky stared. “You went back. You were done. You left me for [REDACTED_NAME] even though you'd say— [REDACTED_NAME] smiled, soft. “I'd be with you 'til the end of the line. Yeah. But the timeline wasn’t.” [REDACTED_NAME] looked at [REDACTED_DEMONYM] Bucky Stan, then at Bucky’s hand intertwined with theirs. “Guess this is where everything finally lines up,” he said. Bucky exhaled, like something heavy finally let go. “I’m not running anymore,” he said. “I’m choosing this.” [REDACTED_NAME] nodded, proud. “Then my job’s done.” The shield vanished. Time stitched itself shut. And just like that, the Winter Soldier didn’t get saved by war or duty—but by an [REDACTED_DEMONYM] Bucky Stan who believed in him, and a best friend who came back through time just to make sure the knot was tied.

... "I should BE the only one in your class... and the only one in your heart. Any... questions? Clarifications?" She whispers to his ear. "Viiiolent reactions?" He did not say a word but patted her shoulder as he pushed her slowly to make breathing room. Her face was puzzled, wondering why he'd do that out of nowhere. But his expression remained relaxed, he was not interested in this game where he thought she was playing. "What makes you think I'm not going to tell this to the administration of your actions?" He calmly asked with a hint of aggression. "What you're doing is a violation to the conduct of this university, and partaking to your actions would be a detriment to my own career and personal life." Confident about his defense, he smirked a little and gave her time to answer before he would leave the room. But in spite of that, her next expression left him worried, as she gradually opened up a big smile, like she was waiting for that prompt of his. "My, my, sir... Didn't you check our Canvas?" "Didn't bother to. Now sit down." "Oho, such assertion!-" "Please. Sit. Down." He declared with more energy in his voice, like a proper professor would. She exhales and submits. "If you say so, Sir~" "And you sit down at the front seats. I don't want to waste my energy projecting my voice to the ends of this classroom." He narrows his eyebrows, his emotionless eyes glaring at the only student." She playfully pouts and and winks back. "Okay~ Now can you look me up?" "What is with you- later okay? Let me just..." He pinches his nose, trying to make note of the situation he's under. "I presume you're some rich girl who's just avid at this sort of subject?" "Fufu... I like it when you're intentionally dense. And- I- know- whenever you're intentionally dense. It all feels like allowance to me." "Am I supposed to know what does that supposed to mean?" He crosses his arms as he starts up his laptop. She chuckles. "You can always take a guess~ I mean, professors like you are more intuitive than students nowadays, hmm? At least that's what the topic is all about whenever I hear the faculty. Us using generative AI to pass our classes in a whim," She says all this while twirling her hair. "colluding secretly, hiding our devices under our legs amidst quizzes- oh the list goes on, Sir!" "Ju- just shut up, hold on- hold on for a minute, okay? It's late, and I'm pissed that only one student was able to enlist here. So, stay quiet and wait!" He checks the members of the class in Canvas. "... You're pissed, huh." "Of course I a-" ... Sudden silence. She then chuckles and crosses her leg assertively, like her presence envelops the classroom all of a sudden. "Oh dear Sir, have you found me already? I wanted to talk more to you just as your student! But... maybe you can call me..." She whispers a bit louder than earlier, wanting him to hear her words. "Your... dearest..." Another long pause. "... This is why the administration had always allowed this, didn't they?" She smiled. "So you're that intuitive... as I thought~"

As a student-leader, I hope the my org mates can take note that, yes, our orgs matter, and we take those commitments seriously. But it seems like we are expected to be everything all at once, and in that pressure, we forget that we are still learning. When something goes wrong, call it out but do it with care. Choose to correct with kindness, to guide instead of shame, and to help people grow rather than break them down. Accountability matters, but so does grace. I’m sure you have done a mistakes as well. Please remember there are real people who are behind the positions. We are students first, before we are student leaders. We are still learning and trying. We are not immune to burnout or failure. We do the best we can with the time, energy, and hearts we have. Holding us to impossible standards, or crucifying us for mistakes, only drains the very people who are trying their best to serve the community they call home. This is not to say we become lenient. But we can set boundaries yet let kindness stay.

Hi. I need to let something out. I used to believe that wanting something badly was enough. That if I wanted it with discipline, sacrifice, and exhaustion, the world would eventually say yes. I was taught that hunger was admirable, that ambition was proof of worth, that the people who made it were simply the ones who never stopped reaching. So I reached. Hard. I rearranged my life around a future that didn’t exist yet. I missed dinners. I delayed conversations. [REDACTED_NAME]. [REDACTED_NAME], may [REDACTED_NAME]. I told myself this was temporary. That absence was just the price of becoming someone. That everyone would understand once I arrived. But now I am sitting with rejection letters instead of success, and the silence after it is unbearable. The nos were polite. Professional. Almost kind. And somehow that made them worse. Proof that wanting, no matter how disciplined, does not guarantee succeed. Proof that effort does not promise entry. Proof that you can give so much of yourself and still be told you are not what they’re looking for. And I keep asking myself: what if I hurt people for a future that never opens its door? At what point does ambition stop being brave and start being cruel? How many people are you allowed to disappoint in the name of becoming? How long can you call distance “focus” before it’s just loss? Behind the recognition I imagined, there is a smaller, quieter want that hurts more than failure: I want one ordinary day back. A day where I was happy without earning it. A day where laughter wasn’t scheduled. A day where love didn’t feel like something I had to postpone until I succeeded. I miss my friends. I miss someone. I miss closeness before it became calculated. I left and hurt people because I believed success would justify the leaving. That one day I’d look back and say, “See? Worth it naman eh.” But what if it isn’t? What if all I’m left with are memories of who I hurt and no proof it led anywhere? I don’t know how to grieve a future that never happened and the people I lost chasing it. I don’t even know what I want anymore. Only that I once loved something deeply, and now even that feels fragile, postponed, unsafe to touch. Something in me refuses to let go of the idea that I am meant for more. I don’t know if that’s resilience or stubbornness. [REDACTED_NAME] or fear. [REDACTED_NAME] or denial. I keep asking questions I don’t yet know how to answer: Can you want greatly without abandoning people? Can you chase a future without disappearing from the present? Can ambition love back? I’m wondering if the life I’m working toward will ever give me back what it already took. And I’m also wondering if wanting something badly was ever the same thing as being meant for it.

Hi! I just want to rant about how I'm feeling about my shifting right now. So, I had gotten an F in calculus which led to me getting kicked out of my original program. Now, I originally wanted to shift into [DEPARTMENT], which I was sure of. But then, my parents brought up LM, and that gave me a headache for a while, because I was thinking of taking up [DEPARTMENT] as my main course, graduate, work for a year, then go to UP law school afterwards. I even told them about my plans to minor/spec on Creative Writing and Financial Management, but then they asked me why not LM? I even tried to do my research on the pros and cons of both courses. I told them because I felt I wasn't ready for the workload of reading several legal texts. They told me about how much I loved reading. But I just couldn't feel it, at least, not right now. They then told me, if I was gonna go to law school, why would I take [DEPARTMENT] then? Especially with the belief that LM was a great prep for that. And how they also felt [DEPARTMENT] just being marketing didn't have the same aura as that of being an architect or engineer. Then they asked me why don't I just consider more courses than just those two? I chose just those two because those were the only ones I felt were stable enough. Then they believed that since I was an [NCR] student, I would be hired regardless, and I just feel like they don't quite understand. They even suggested that I take a course that was easy enough, so that I would graduate with Latin honors. But I felt I would feel unfulfilled by that, and would destroy me in the real world. As far as I know, tomorrow is the last day of re-advisement, which is why me trying to decide on my course is giving me a massive headache. And I feel lost. I just feel lost. And I didn't get mad at them. I felt stoically furious because I felt like I was unable to examine how I really felt... Rant over.

Given naman na for those well-off (though, I'm not generalising for ALL ah), especially those who grew up in their own bubbles without being emersed to something other than what fits their societal standards and norms, but to think that those academically gifted na proud maging [REDACTED_NAME] sila would be just as superficial? [REDACTED_NAME] wdym you'll only surround yourself with people who are like you and have benefits to you, that your so-called friends are determined by how their abilities ease your academics? Self-preservation is a normal and necessary thing, yes, but that shouldn't cancel out the other important parts of our humanity that enrich good morals and authentic experiences like showing a bit of kindness to people who are struggling and may not be as capable or privileged as you at the moment and literally just not judging a book by its cover. Sometimes the assumptions that we have of people hinder us from encountering real things, and our hesitancy to confront or embrace something different hinder us from experiencing actual growth. I'm so disappointed with what we've become as a society. I really hope this changes because I've personally been through a lot amd still go through them each day, but this community that I'm supposed to be a part of makes me feel ignored and even condemned, because I'm not allowed the privilege to be seen and felt welcome here. This feeling haunts me every day I've gone to school to see my peers, and I don't think I can handle it anymore.

Now that the funeral's over, and we're just arranging the small business our dad left, I really need an avenue to talk about it. How the emotional clashed with the bureaucracy of things. My mind still went back to my experience in the ER when I saw dad being revived, in between life and death, then suddenly the doctor set me aside to sign a medico-legal letter. Imagine the emotional and mental torture. There's a clashing. I'm not asking for psychological help, but it would be nice to have a philosophy professor to talk to lang to help me make sense of this. BUT with this certain groupmate, all the information needed is already there, like everything is in the masterfile or mainly in the chat. But for their side of the story, I apparently hadn't told them certain info/details or I'm not being "transparent" enough, hence that being a reason for them not really doing their work. I know everyone has their side of the story, but I'm looking at timestamps, seeing it was already sent in advance, and to add, they already reacted to it and made a comment (kinda rude) about the details, which proved that I did provide it, and that they acknowledged it. Like I get it if you didnt have a chance to read it or even simply forgot, but I just don't like that I'm being treated as the problem, or them not simply apologizing. (and to be clear, this happened multiple times) And I get it, I'm not perfect. I make mistakes. But I'm also not someone who caves in so easily, I don't want to be pushed around. I know the truth. That why I don't know if they're gaslighting me or them just forgetting or if somehow I'm the problem? And what's worse, apparently from another groupmate/people, they wanted to report me and some others? Like did they do much of the work? They promised many things, but took so long or even eventually didn't do it. And yet again, I'm the one to blame. Even the reason for the report was kinda funny lol. And to make it even worse, someone did call them out actually, and it was calm, without much blame actually, but she took it as an insult and with much offense. Even putting words into someone else's mouth. And to add, even if we try to do their parts, they still somehow rejects it most of the time lol, so it was really exhausting. Again, I know everyone has a side to their story, but it really feels like I can debunk every single claim they made about me or my groupmates. Its like having all the worst traits of a groupmate of being rude, incompetent, and a lowkey freeloader. They for sure can do some things well, but the negatives really outweigh the positives, and my entire group actually felt the same. Even like some outsiders, who've worked with them, said similar things. At the end of the day, Idk if its a work ethic issue, or her just being a tough person to work with. But I do know, that I know my worth and while not perfect, I did my work. If just in the case, I do have receipts hihi Anyways, just a PSA to choose groupmmates wisely, and protect your mental health. I wish I was more assertive and didn't let them crush me or disrespect me (and even some other members of the group), and that I will admit is a fault of mine. I was too worried of peer evals. But now I just want to let it all out that the sem is done. k

A reply to Love is not tolerating error and justifying what is error as true. That is rationalism. Your definition of “love” is akin to “tolerance” and “inclusion”. But true and real love, in a Catholic sense, wants and wills the good of the other. This is called “agape” or “agapic love”. I love you, and because I love you, I want what is truly good for you. I want what will actually lead you to your final end. I want you to get to Heaven and be with God forever. Good parents treat their children with discipline and firmness but also with compassion and care. If the latter two outweigh the former two, they will be raising spoiled brats. We have too many of those already. You may ask: do children enjoy being raised with discipline and firmess? Of course not. Will they, on their own accord, eat the brocolli and cabbage over ice cream and candy? Will they instantly come to know that a good grade in school must be earned and studied for? Of course not. They are children. On the other hand, are the veggies necessary? Is the hardwork necessary for the A? Of course they are. That is what real love is. It sometimes has be firm because firmness is needed in instilling in the child that a balanced diet is needed, or that hardwork is necessary to get the A. Then, hopefully with time, the children develop those habits and no longer do the parents have to be so firm anymore. Are tolerance and inclusion of all people, especially the marginalized, part of Catholic love? Of course they are. No question there. Should we shout to LGBTQ people that they are going to Hell? Of course not. Only God knows the human heart in its entirety. No [REDACTED_NAME] should ever take the place of God as the ultimate judge of souls. On the other hand, should the Catholic Church remain firm in preaching Catholic moral truths? Definitely. Not to oppress, but to lead to salvation. Because true love wants your ultimate good: your salvation. —College [REDACTED_DEMONYM] (in case you’re wondering)

I think we have to have a conversation about what makes the "performative male" joke, well, "performative". It's not that having all these things immediately makes you performative. It's when you PERFORM by pretending to like things that you don't really like to try and make yourself look better (usually to the opposite sex). If you legitimately believe in things and are just honest and openly express it, it's not "performative" in this sense. (Though, of course, we can also push this further and talk about the performativity of gender in general, but anyway)

Warning: Lengthy Rant Goodbye, [NCR] To begin, thank you for one wonderful semester. Thank you for being my second home, in this very short period of time. I need terror profs to really internalize the fact that for every student that they fail for not meeting their high standards means so much more than just simply losing Latin Honors. A failed class is 15k pesos for that class down the drain. A failed class could mean dropping out of [NCR] entirely due to a lost scholarship because they can’t afford to go otherwise. A failed class could mean an extra semester of even more fees outside of the units to pay for. A failed class could mean the inability to shift to another course due to being unable to meet a QPI threshold. That’s not even mentioning how emotionally taxing having a failed class can be or how becoming irregular student generally makes your academic life significantly worse. Nobody is asking you to give incompetent students a participation trophy, but it wouldn’t kill you to show more consideration for your students who have to balance your class alongside others and their own issues in their private lives. There can only be so much “your QPI doesn’t matter” before it starts becoming a life ruining situation.

Prof here. I’m thankful to the op who wrote . And I agree that profs must be treated with more respect. No that doesn’t mean you have to call me ma’am or sir in every single sentence. But I’m uncomfortable with the impression that this post can give: we aren’t helpless victims begging for money. We are human beings who are working, just like everyone else. Just like you college kids who will be or are job hunting. The impact good profs can make is inestimable; I really wish we can focus on that more. They are remembered by their students for years, and even decades to come. I wish we focus on that aspect rather than focusing/stereotyping/restricting profs to the “kulang ng sahod” label. We have much more dignity than that.

Here's the thing about AI. At some point in history, the humble calculator was once novel. I'm definitely too young to remember if its reception was also met with suspicion. But now, the calculator, even the scientific calculator, is widely accepted everywhere in society. Even in math classes. So, to those who want absolutely nothing to do with AI, beware: your time is coming to an end. Should we use AI for everything? Should it be a substitute to write our academic papers? Absolutely not. That must be clear. Can it assist us in finding sources, like journal articles, online? Definitely. If you have a problem with this, again, your time is coming to an end. You are probably someone who hates the fact that students now can find sources much easier, when you had to do everything manually back then. Be happy for others. Why deprive them of a good?

Prof here. I want to give a more nuanced view on the whole salary issue. We’re not eating from the trash, fyi. We also have cars, a house, fly on airplanes, etc. I saw this elderly guy’s post about him flying to New York City on a business class cabin. (Who knows if that was his money, or sponsored by a university, or something?) So, why the un-nuanced view about profs having “low salaries”. Here’s the thing. The higher you are in academic standing, the higher one’s salary is. So many really work toward the goal of getting a doctorate, publish journal articles, and so on. A general rule: a prof’s salary is probably lower than someone working at P&G. (Although, again, this depends on the prof’s academic credentials and such) BUT: profs can have an INCREDIBLY laid back life, IF they are efficient with their time. If you work corporate, you’re stuck with a 9-5 and have to go to the office no matter what. FYI, profs do NOT get paid less when classes suspend (which are many times). We do NOT get paid less for every president’s day, study day, etc. That’s why, dear students: we earn the same whether you give your best in our classes or not. Our salaries do not suffer; only you end up wasting your tuition money. (Unfortunately, I cannot say the same about the rest of our kababayans who are no work, no pay) Oh and about the business class thing.. [NCR] actually sponsors our trips to local and overseas conferences. Sure, funding can be way more abundant, but the point is that at least the money is there for us to use. I know many colleagues who have been beneficiaries of a fully-funded international conference attendance. Yes, my salary might be less than a P&G person, but I am not stressed. I do not let bad students get to me — nor again do they harm my salary, unfortunately for them! Why do many profs speak out about low salaries? This is just my suspicion: they do NOT manage their time efficiently and so they FEEL that the work they give out is not proportionate to what they are receiving as salary. Stuck in old ways of teaching perhaps? Of course, this is just one way to look at it. I am unmarried and the salary is all mine at the end of the day. Whenever there is a sudden suspension because of bad weather, for example, I do not go on zoom or google meet, unlike some profs who insist on it. I record my lecture for that day and just post it for my classes to watch on their own time. This is both efficient AND student learning-centered. Nor do I give inefficiently give 10,000 diff assessments throughout the sem when in fact, two summative assessments and class participation can, well, efficiently encapsulate learning. What’s the moral of what I want to say? Profs need to be EFFICIENT and at the same time maximize student learning. It is possible. If you think it is not, then you are stuck in the past in a bad way.

This sem taught me a lot about group work — especially about people who only show up when there’s an audience. It’s hard working with someone who talks loudly about “urgency” but disappears when things are actually being done behind the scenes. Someone who criticizes others for side comments (ones that even aren't that bad) but doesn’t notice their own. It’s frustrating when you try to communicate, adjust, and be patient, but the person who needs to listen the most is also the one who listens the least. And honestly, the pattern never changed, the delays, the defensiveness, the blaming, the lack of follow-through. Like even when called out nicely, like there wasn't even blame on our part to her, but they turned themselves into the victim, and put words into other's mouths. I promise we have been super patient with them, but they barely moved and they really shifted all the blame to the group for their incompetency. Like many of the things he blamed us for, I can attest are mostly or even all untrue. They just dont listen/ What’s worse is when they act like they’re carrying the whole group, but you only see them active pag presentation na or when they need something approved. Meanwhile, everyone else has been consistently working the entire time. Then when things aren’t done their way or their time, suddenly “walang urgency” daw, even if the work was already moving. I’m just glad the semester is done. *insert chains free meme* Not to be harsh, but I never want to work with again, and how leadership is more than talking, criticizing, and showing up only when there’s a spotlight. Real work happens in the parts no one sees, and some people will never understand that. People do tell me I've been too patient with him or even too nice in general, but I can't anymore. But hey... in the real world, you don't always get to choose who you work with, and that's still something we all have to learn. Though still let yourself keep the peace.

To incoming freshies/aspiring [REDACTED_NAME] who plan to pursue corporate, please get a math-related degree. Recruiters look for graduates of management engineering (for management, consultancy) and economics (for finance, banking). To fellow [REDACTED_NAME] who are already in non-math-related courses and are planning to pursue law, do org work. Pad your resume with leadership experience and at least two internships, and take your chances with a big MNC, FMCG, or any well-known company. Learn how to interview, practice your analytical and financial skills, and by God take accounting seriously. I'm telling you this as a senior who didn't take a math-related degree and followed my dreams (lol), didn't get a lot of org/internship experience, and is currently struggling to find work in this economy. Yes, it's competitive out there. Some people are going to disagree, but the reality is that most companies don't even get the chance to see you for you. They filter applicants based on how stellar their resumes appear. If you had a different experience, congrats, you're lucky. But let's be real: that's not going to be true for most. If you're aspiring for big, then ngayon pa lang, be it. I made the mistake of being too lax with org & leadership experience, and also my internship. Please don't be like me. By all means, enjoy your stay in [NCR], but also, make sure to prepare for real life as you graduate and get ready for work. If I could only turn back time :(

Bakit yung other countries umaasenso, tapos Philippines hirap pa rin? One angle to think about it, maybe, can be boiled down to one word: AUTHENTICITY. Think about other countries: Japan, South Korea, Thailand, Vietnam. We crave REAL Japanese food. It doesn’t have to be super expensive, pero gusto natin yung quality sushi and sashimi — [REDACTED_NAME] rice na konti lang ang fish. We also crave AUTHENTIC pho — yung may maraming veggies at tunay na lasa, hindi yung parang watered-down na version na puro sabaw lang. [REDACTED_NAME], they really prioritize quality, kaya EVEN street food nila, gustong-gusto ng rich Pinoys when they travel there. No shame in buying street food. We want real street food, hindi yung puro MSG at fillers. Kaya pag nasa Thailand or Singapore tayo, diretso tayong street food. Pero dito sa Pilipinas, parang di natin magawa. Some will probably even think of it as shameful to buy from just a karinderia or street vendor. Other countries keep things real, authentic, and high quality, [REDACTED_NAME] laging mahal. Eh tayo? If we really want the country to succeed, businesses should stop thinking only about how to make the most profit. Dapat iniisip din ang AUTHENTICITY and QUALITY. [REDACTED_NAME] mindset [REDACTED_NAME], maybe [REDACTED_NAME] talaga [REDACTED_NAME] real chance to move forward. [REDACTED_NAME], ang gubyerno ng Pilipinas ay may malaking role sa endeavor na ito. LOVE THE PHILIPPINES, as the tourism tagline goes. INVEST IN IT, AND DO SO WITH QUALITY IN MIND.

[NCR] is a Jesuit school, and thus fundamentally rooted at its core as a liberal arts university. It is a place for the free discussion of ideas, opinions, and beliefs. Thus, it is here that I wish to share an opinion where others may be receptive to it and challenge it: Communism is a religion founded upon the fundamentally gnostic belief of dialectics. It has its rituals, community, and metaphysics. It has its god, its messiah, its heaven, its chosen people, and its inferno. Like other religions, it splinters into its cults and sects of which preach absolute piety and fidelity while brutally punishing apostasy. It is a religion that aims to subjugate the human person under the totalitarian control of the managerial class, its trade commissars, its high-priests. It is built upon circular logic of definitions defining definitions based on nothing in order to justify its logos. It uses sophistry and rhetoric as a tool to decieve the human being into its honeypot. Its ultimate goal is to hijack the working class' productivity by robbing the individual of their inherently unequal, high agency personhood through converting them into an indistinguishable number. This is an ideo-religion that promises 'freedom' by enacting absolute slavery to the state. Once the totalitarian state has enacted full communism, "the state will wither away" and bring about utopia. This is a lie, a deception to use you and eventually control you. Communism desires to do away with the individual, in order to bring about a full, new world order. This is reflected in its way of attempting to centrally plan the fundamentally inequal human person. It is also reflected in its way to deceive the innately discernment of value through its baseless labour theory of value (assigning made up numbers to control the markets). This is a theory which has created this corrupted economic system that is at the whims of the central banking system (a system created to hijack value) that prints infinite currency to rob people of the value of their money through inflation. Lord [REDACTED_NAME] lied, more government spending will not grow the economy, it will only be used to grow our rulers' accounts. It is an ideo-religion that forgets the ugliness and inherent differences found inside the human being, and ignores the evidence that is absolutely observable in the history of all human civilization. It then formulates the idea of a 'socially engineered' golem known as the 'blank slate' which it claims is applicable to the demographics of all people. It then creates a perfect system based on this assumption. It then creates an internal logic to protect itself from the critical by defining itself as successful. Thus, when it eventually fails "it wasn't real communism/socialism" becomes the excuse. Communism, and its priests, uses rhetoric and ever changing definitions to mask itself. However, fundamentally it is undeniably a type of socialism. Socialism is a system designed to create processes of control over the individual. It is State control over the means of production. State control over the productive, producing individual. These 'progressives', these 'nazis', these 'fascists', these 'communists', all of them are sects of socialism competing over the same ground. Ask this question: "why do all of these seem to have similarities?", to which you will ultimately conclude that 'They are all fundamentally the same.' They simply, differ in means and their vocabularies. They speak another language, an ideological one that is ever shifting. One that is similiar to those evident in esoteric traditions. They will try to deceive you by taking control of definitions, by trying to screen and conjure confusions (by claiming things as fluid), all to avoid being nailed down and pressed for the truth, the Yes or No. But, if you have already discerned and know the truth: they are all similiar in that they are all trying to enslave you. "If you want a picture of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face—forever" Socialists subscribe to and obsess about race theory and social darwinism (though they deny it, because of the history of ideas, and to deceive you). It is an attempt to categorize the individual beings into a group, so as to easier manipulate and influence them into acting in ways that can be measured and predicted. It was not the individual persons (the class consciousness) who invented socialism but the aspiring managerial class who think themselves smarter than you, who think they know better for you than you yourself. They don't want to change the system to free you, they just want to be the ones running it. Once you begin seeing and treating people as unique individuals and not groups, then you will realize that ideas of race is a fabrication made to manipulate you (genetics and heredity is an entirely different debate). Look around you, discern, and you shall 'Know' the truth. Your circumstances and your parents synthesized you, your personality, your demeanor, your attitudes, all of your person is a result of a culmination of inequal and inconceivably complex factors of unpredictable and irrational degrees. You are not centrally planned, you are not socially engineered, you are an individual being with an individual consciousness that is manifested by the thousands upon thousands of free beings who have come before you. You are blessed and gifted with a truly mysterious thing called free-will, a consciousness given to you by the universe. Your life is fundamentally unfair, but this is the kindest formulation the unvierse had in store for you. You are an alive being that must adapt and decide whether it will succeed ot fail in this noisy world. Both notions of which are defined by you and only you, not social pressures, and certainly not the managerial class. Do not allow the managers, the want-to-be high-priests, to deceive you and enslave you. You must defy the machine and the system, the inhuman thing that is attempting to trick you into willingly putting yourself into its chains. It, like a certain organized religion of the Abrahamic faith, has used the powerful notions of 'Struggle', oppression of a people, and the hope of a promised land (utopia) in order to grow its following and motivate its sheep. It uses these to deceive you into giving away your individuality, so that they can eventually slaughter you with impunity in the name of doing the 'greatest good' for the 'greatest number'. You may follow this ideo-religion most likely because of your fear of independence. You follow because innately you want to be taken cared of and you want to belong. For the religious, it is the vain attempt of returning to the garden of Eden. The managerial class know this and thus take advantage of you. They promise that by giving away your freedoms you will become free. They use bright colors and your innate idealism to corrall you. They use kindly words to sedate you. Binangkal Recipe Ingredients: 1 1/2 cup all purpose flour 3/4 cup powdered milk 1 tablespoon baking powder 1/3 cup sugar 1 tablespoon melted butter (or margarine) 1/4 cup evaporated milk 1 piece egg 1/2 cup sesame seeds Oil for deep frying Directions: 1. In a large bowl, add the all purpose flour, powdered milk and baking powder. Combine and mix well. 2. In another bowl, combine the sugar, evaporated milk, melted butter and egg. Mix until well combined. 3. Add the wet mixture to the dry mixture. Mix until well combined. Knead for a few minutes until smooth and sticky. 4. Grab a small portion of the mixture and then roll into a ball. Then, coat them with sesame seeds. 5. When fully covered by sesame seeds, roll them again in your palm to make sure that the seeds will not fall. Do these steps with the rest of the ingredients. Heat a lot of oil in a pan. Deep fry the binangkal balls until golden brown. Transfer them to a strainer or paper towel to drain excess oil. Transfer the binangkal to a serving plate. She gasped in surprise. “W-what?...” she mumbled. Then, it twisted. She felt a rush of agony go through her. “Ahh!” She screamed. She began to feel lightheaded as the pain increased. Just then, the knife was removed. The pain was worse than anything she had ever experienced. She inhaled, making it hurt even worse. She sat her hand on her stomach and felt the sensation of irritation. She lifted her hand to her face. On the surface of her palm was a red liquid. “blood?...” she said, her stomach throbbing with discomfort with every breath. Her head started to pound even worse. She couldn’t understand what had just happened. It was too much to comprehend for her weak self. She turned her head, then her body, she caught a glance of you. Next thing she knew, she couldn’t stand. She stumbled over, falling to the ground. She hit the ground hard, sending a shockwave of pain throughout her body. Then, she lay there. Knowing that the one that had stabbed her was someone she knew as one of her kind. As she lay there, bleeding out, she took time to acknowledge that the person that stabbed her was another human, just as she was, and yet she knew what had happened, she simply couldn’t understand why. Why someone would do this to her, to anyone as a matter of fact. All she knew was that she was going to die alone, on the floor with no one there. Nobody to hold her hand, and nobody to care for her. She had a very unpleasant expression on her face, a mix of confusion, sadness, and fear, yet she was not angry, nor happy. Just pure despair and uncertainty. Not knowing what to think of you now. Except...she knew that she was going to die. She knew that there was no saving her and that it was over. And finally...she looked at you, and with her last breath. She said “[REDACTED_NAME] did you just twist the knife?” 🕴️🕴️🕴️🕴️🕴️ miss 💔 ko 👇🏿 na 🚡 [REDACTED_NAME] 🌈. if 😶 you 🙌 don't ❌ mind 🧠 me 👇🏿, magrerelapse🎧 [REDACTED_NAME] 👇🏽 saglit. 🏎 Sigma sigma 67 🕡 🕖 🖐️☝️🖐️✌️

Before any of you start bashing this post without anything of substance, read it first and think twice. I find it extremely ironic that the school itself, a Catholic institution, creates, promotes, and supports LGBTQ initiatives. If the school wasn't religiously affiliated at all, then I would have no problem with it, but the fact that the school claims to uphold Catholic values while simultaneously promoting LGBTQ ideas is just baffling to me. If they simply just claimed to respect the orientations of the people/students in the LGBTQ, I wouldn't have an issue at all as the catechism of the Catholic Church teaches that the orientation itself is not sinful. However, the fact that they have events such as the "Transgender Day of Remembrance" promoting the idea of transgenderism which is considered sinful in the church, is bitterly ironic. You can definitely respect the people in that community as we are all taught that Christ loves all of us unconditionally, but loving a person doesn't mean you're going to condone their sinful actions. inb4 everyone in the comment section starts accusing me of homophobia, transphobia and hate without even reading what i wrote ;))

I don’t deserve to be at [NCR]. I often question myself: Why am I even here? Why was I accepted into [NCR] only for me to feel like some kind of joke? I feel so stupid every single day. I can’t even bring myself to recite in class, even though I’m well aware that class participation is a very important component of my grade. I’m barely surviving academically. I’m falling behind. Everyone else seems to be doing well in their classes and is so much more literate. I don’t understand why I took this program. Ever since the first day, I’ve felt like I never truly belonged. To make matters worse, I don’t have much in common with any of my blockmates. I hate how much of a coward I am. I barely interact with any of them—too anxious about what they think of me, too scared they’ll be weirded out, and that I’m too much of a social kill to be breathing the same air whenever I’m near them. I constantly cry in my locked room every day after I come home from campus. I despise how my anxiety takes over every time I encounter even the smallest inconveniences. I overthink everything that happened during the day. I panic, and suddenly I’m on the floor, gasping for air, clutching my chest, trying to get a hold of myself. I never get to open up with anybody in fear of judgment. I isolate myself from everyone, suffering through my internal demons alone. My mind is always on a blur and fogged up. I can’t process and think properly. I’m genuinely getting worse and worse as the days continue passing by. Sometimes, I wished I passed along with them. The rope just might end up winning this time; it definitely will. - soss freshie

I don’t know where else to turn and I’ve been feeling just awful lately. But since it’s trans awareness month I feel I can open up. I’ve always felt like I was born in the wrong body, that what I feel on the inside didn’t match what I am on the outside. Sometimes the dysphoria is so bad that I just want to curl up into a ball and cry. No one around me understands, not my friends or even family. I’ve always felt like a royal even though I was born middle class. I opened up to my friends that my preferred titles are “your highness/your grace” but they don’t get it. My parents are even worse. When I told them that I’m transroyal they told me to just accept myself as the commoner that I was born as and constantly deadclass me. They keep telling me that facts don’t care about your feelings.They don’t know how much emotional pain it causes me when they say that. I tried to educate them by telling them that royalty is a social construct and that you don’t need to be born royal to identify as one but they still refuse to even use my preferred titles. At the moment I’ve saving bits of my allowance so I could buy a crown. It’s pretty expensive but it would really help me appear the way I feel inside. It’s like that song “Golden”, “I didn’t know how to believe I was the queen that I’m meant to be.”

Controversial post! Am I the only one who finds those people who immediately request a change in class modality, once they find out that there will be rallies, somewhat disrespectful? Before anything else, I hold the same political stance as the majority do. Of course, no one supports corruption, unless you are one of them🙄. However, if you are clearly not part of the inconvenienced group by the rally and you won’t be attending the event either because you do not feel like it or whatever, then why not just request the prof to excuse those won’t be able to come? Either way, people will choose to abuse this freedom to decide whether to pasok or not. The difference is we are not taking advantage of the professor’s kindness. I am pretty sure most of these people also hold the same views as us, anyway, so they will understand. For the non-thinking human beings who might react to this post impulsively, OBVIOUSLY, if your reasons are valid and you are not doing it for your personal benefit (e.g., to accommodate one’s laziness), then it shall be considered as an exemption. DUH! There’s no if you just use your brain, especially logic. Anyway, that’s all

hello everyone! i have been wanting to put myself out there, so as a form of my "exposure therapy" of sorts, i would like to do a meetup for at least 5 students from as much campuses as i can. context: i am diagnosed with asd and ocd since last year, and while my uni has orgs, i feel like i am limited in what i do and i feel like everyone has their own circle and i could just not say a word in any of their convos. it's best that i am doing this outside of my university so i cannot put an excuse that people "already know each other" so i could talk to them. so what do i want to do? i would like to have at least 5 students and we would meet up at a nearby coffee shop and have a talk of sorts. a 1 hour meet up at minimum. where: any chill coffeeshop nearby your campus would do when: on a wednesday afternoon (for this semester), to be planned who: undergrad/grad students/alumni... let's make this diverse!! goal: make sure i get to know all 5 NEW people, and talk about topics that are either fun or substantial.. or both also, just for warning, i might vomit or have difficulty breathing, but that's what i'm trying to overcome. i hope it doesn't turn out all bad. i hope that before this year ends, i would be able to overcome one of my biggest fears and be able to push myself a little bit further from where i am today. and while i can help myself, i think the weight is lightened if i know that i am not in this journey alone. please comment if you are interested or have any suggestions 🫶 i will pm and make ourselves a group chat if possible thank you very much huhuhu

The Guidon should investigate the Blue Babble Battalion. It’s a team chronically underfunded and undersupported especially during CDC. They lack adequate coaching and conditioning staff, have no dedicated dance coach, and still train on worn-out blue mats. The story should ask: Why the history of last-place finishes? Why are skilled members leaving? Why has the program been allowed to decline this far, and what’s blocking real improvement and progress? What worked well in the previous programs and what’s not working today? This isn’t about blaming the athletes. They continue to show up and perform despite the odds. It’s about holding the institution accountable and sparking a conversation on what needs to change. The Blue Babble Battalion deserves more than cheers from the sidelines. They deserve real support.

It disgusts me how even the Catholic Church antagonizes activists. Framing their "puro mga rally" activities as maladaptive coping is a grossly misinformed perspective. Why do people treat us criminals when the work they do is NO DIFFERENT from the BS Org Life? We network, we schedule activities, we plan projects. [REDACTED_NAME] targets for guest speakers, pag may pushbacks, etc. The risk of burnout is real, but it's the same for EVERY PURSUIT we chose to take. So why are activists conveniently "othered"? I don't understand why being "problem-focused" is bad, because how else do you solve a problem other than to face it head-on? We can't just wait for things to fall into place through God's intervention or smth when we, in the image and likeness of God, literally have the collective power to exact justice. Instead, we'd rather roleplay a God that tolerates all kinds of violence, so long as it does not personally affect us. How can you preach compassion when the cup you offer is laced with the poison of prejudice?

Anyone who shotgunned both the [REDACTED_DEMONYM] math and numerical subtests and still got in? My prior course is philo and i didnt shotgun both of those subtests but i still feel anxious. I was running on 4 hours of sleep on the day i took the exam because i was cramming on math several days leading up to my test date. I went to a school with very lax standards so i graduated from highschool barely knowing any math and the first thing i did when i started studying math was to teach myself pemdas. I was sure about my answers for most of the algebra questions, half of the geometry questions, and none for trig. For the other sections I feel most confident about my answers on abstract reasoning, okayish on english, reading comprehension, and analogy; and lastly i have no idea how well i did on logical reasoning because all i could think of at the time while reading the questions was “honestly it depends 😭” i either aced it or scored very low. I walked out of the test room feeling tired hungry and defeated. And till now a few weeks later after i took the test i still feel like shitting myself sometimes wondering if i did well enough to be accepted, especially because i dont have the best grades nor the most well written essay, im mediocre on those fronts too.

There is absolutely ZERO reason for AISIS to still be this garbage after years and years of experiencing the same issues. At this point, it's just disappointing to see that the school I dreamed of being in is now turning into a glorified mess.

Need to rant too hard, but while I understand that obviously “freeloaders” make group work stressful — the overly hounding ones are another topic. Like, I get it, you want things done and organized, but being passive-aggressive about it every few hours doesn’t help anyone. Honestly, just want to say, I may not have seen the messages because I had class or it was a Sunday. It doesn’t mean I’m freeloading. Like it’s LITERALLY just been a few hours or maybe just the weekend — stop trying to make it look like we’re deliberately ignoring or freeloading. I know you want work done, but not everyone has the same schedule. And I do look at the timestamps, and I do try my best to reflect, and I will admit if I had something wrong. (Though I do sometimes apologize for the sake of apologizing hihi.) But looking at most of these timestamps, barely any time has passed, and they really make it look like we’re completely in the wrong. And some of them are just painfully passive-aggressive with their comments — like those being really nice, but implying. Some even go to the point of lowkey threatening the situation by saying that theyll reach out to the prof or something. Giving those long-ass messages of disappointment. I mean like, there are all those different standards but theres just that lack of nuance, and coming to those conclusions, and kind of making the others who do work feel a little infrerior. And as mentioned, yes we all have different viewpoints, but its also really exhausting sometimes. I do feel like these people are perfectionist types overall, but understand that I do pull my weight. Even though I’m not active all the time, my schedule is honestly quite packed, and I admit my outputs aren’t as good as they used to be and I’m feeling burnt out — but at the end of the day, I still pull my weight. And the passive-aggressiveness while trying to look nice isn’t nice. Just please act nice-r. And communicate much better. Understand that everyone has their time. Also, small side note — I once saw someone reply “walang response??” the same literal minute after sending their first message. Like not even the next minute. Like girl. 💀 I’ll save a whole post for this person when the sem or maybe year is over. [REDACTED_NAME] there's a lot hahaha Anyway, just a gentle reminder: collaboration ≠ control. [REDACTED_NAME]. We’re all doing our best (I think). 😅 This isn’t to excuse freeloaders ofc, but yeah, it’s part of life.

I was in Shopwise grocery in Commonwealth Ave. very recently. Great place. It used to look old and rundown, but renovations have made it much more aesthetically appealing. Then comes something that welcomed me: aisles and aisles of FOREIGN GOODS. Chips, goodies, instant noodles, candies, you name it. Now that’s completely fine. But what I was very disheartened about was that these endless aisles all had the tag “IMPORTED” in them. Don’t look anywhere else, THESE are guaranteed imported. And this, ladies and gents, is the problem with the Philippines. Very small thing such as a grocery, but even there, what is prioritized, what is made to stand out, what is given prominence is the FOREIGN, the IMPORTED. Why don’t we share the same passion for things that are actually Philippine-made? Sad to say, many foreign things do outcompete Filipino products. That is why we REALLY need to promote QUALITY, AUTHENTIC products that come from nowhere else but the Philippines. Then [REDACTED_NAME] makausad [REDACTED_NAME].

"Don't you understand, Sir?" She closes the wooden door of the classroom and locks it, leaving both the threatened professor and the forward student closing their distance. "All that you heard from your peers, those itty-bitty... remarks, I call it, of yours... hihi... All those reviews you might have heard from another... er... unassuming party that doesn't matter in both of our eyes... those warnings, those side-eyes, those whispers, those gossips-" The young professor almost trips on the wooden riser as he tries to make respectful distance. "I- I don't know what you mean, but I don't want to assume the worst about you. But you're not- I mean you're not getting into the point of what you're trying to say, unless that's what you want to do with me. Can you... stay back?" "Oh?" She tilts her head. "My, my, assuming the worst about me? That's... you've..." She whispers, "You've really exceeded that expectation of mine... fufu..." "What?" "Anyways, as I was saying. I placed those negative reviews of yours on Profs to Pick. I called you out false, but not scandalous rumors about you not being the best professor, whether by a life lesson or an easy A/B+. Just so that no one would try to pick you." The professor took a sharp breath upon hearing those words. Even if he was only there for 2 years, this type of experience would never happen even if one had been there for 15. "I also had the courtesy to tinker with the AISIS enlistment too! You're thinking now to yourself, 'well, surely other students aside from this wonderful girl of mine would come in right?' But that's tooo bad! I granted other professors extra slots gradually, and no one seemed to bat an eye... because like I said. They don't want you. And I made sure they wouldn't want you. Only I do." He gulped as she finally pins him to the whiteboard. "I should BE the only one in your class... and the only one in your heart. Any... questions?"

a weird part of growth i feel no one really talks about: to learn to sit with the silence, and go with the motions of life while still having the peace and maturity to look forward to what will be oftentimes, i notice that i struggle to sit still and bask in life's presence as it comes. "so what's next? what's the answer? what do i do with it?" but why do i need an answer? why must it be immediate? then it hit me. because silence to me is pain, as it is like an unpredictable bamboo that can whip me anytime without my predicting. and like any other human — i dont like having to deal with anything painful. but then i realized... sometimes choosing what's better for you involves pain. and we have pain because growth comes with change. and its part of the process. i really cant always know the next move. the next thing life throws at me. i can only sit still and face life as it comes. and there is no need to do more, because what life throws at me is somehow probably meant to be mine. - so knowing this... can we then say that we wont need to mourn what isnt meant to be ours? - because if its meant to be yours, it will be. - so maybe God isnt calling a certain thing into action yet because it's redirection. it's wisdom. because as His child – what is mine will not be a counterfeit. it will come with peace, and clarity. and i suppose... such is the manifold peace, love, and grace of God. how odd is the call of peace and how it reveals itself to us. - m

I’ve hit a wall, and I could use some help from my fellow [REDACTED_NAME]. I was born lucky, luckier than most. Well-off parents, dream school, size-able inheritance. Thanks to my family, I’ve never known any true moment of poverty or hardship, and probably never will. And as I continue down the current trajectory of my life, I can already see my future slowly unfurl before my eyes. A mansion in a private subdivision. Kids who attend elite private schools. Yearly international vacations. Maybe golf on the weekends. The Filipino dream, served on a silver platter. Please don’t mistake me. I understand that so many people would do anything to have the future that I’m about to have, or to give said future to their own children. But then, why does it fill me with so much dread? Instead of a dream, that future feels like some hedonistic fantasy land, where the only purpose is to take pleasure in the privilege and excesses of one’s life until they’re six feet under. I don’t know what I want anymore, and I could use some perspective or advice.
