Not once have I ever spoken out on this FW, for I honestly don't see the point of airing out my feelings to a whole community of people I probably have never interacted with in my life, and vice versa. But after the onslaught of recent events, and with no one really to talk to about the severity of the situation for lack of relatability, I feel like this is my only way for me to sleep peacefully tonight, if I even can. As someone whose permanent residence is currently in Cebu and whose immediate family and large majority of my dearest friends presently reside there, I have to say, survivor's guilt hurts - a little bit too much, at that. While I've been able to spend most of my time here in [REDACTED_NAME] completely unscathed from the light-to-moderate rains that occasionally hit [NCR], my family and friends have been in the forefront of some of the largest calamities our country has faced this year. First a 6.9-magnitude earthquake, and now just recently, a full-scale typhoon. It really hurts me that while I've been able to enjoy the benefits of being a campus dormer (i.e., continuous electricity, internet, signal, with a good supply of food and water just within arm's reach), my family and friends have been forced to stay on high-alert since the devastating earthquake that hit northern Cebu last September. Some were forced to evacuate their homes during the wee hours of the night, walking down tens of flights of stairs. Some were unable to sleep for a while out of severe fright and trauma. No onsite classes until now since the beginning of October as a safety precaution, like a version of localized lockdown. I even heard from my mother, who just got her signal back after a day of no contact, that my school in Cebu was heavily flooded, and it hurts for me to see how certain areas in my old campus that I used to enjoy strolling around during sunset are now fully covered in a thick blanket of floodwater. With severed trees and fallen leaves looking like a bunch of lost canoes and rubber ducks. But the most painful news I've heard tonight? Two of my beloved teachers residing in Consolacion just lost their house, swept away in a large wave of water. Nothing was saved, nothing. Except their lives. They managed to evacuate before the floods came around. It really pains me to see how much of my home has been reduced to shambles over the past couple of months I've been away. Many of my dear friends in Cebu tell me that they're grateful that I wasn't there to experience the atrocities they've been through, and that it eases their worries immensely to know that I'm doing safe and sound. But that's the thing. With Cebu now being declared in a "state of calamity", and with all the disasters rising in number and intensity, I am never safe from the overwhelming thoughts that one day soon enough, I'll lose them. All of them. The very people who journeyed with me through most, if not all, my significant moments growing up, as well as walked and stayed with me through the darkest moments of my life. To say that our government has been doing what it can to "control" and "alleviate" the situation is an understatement. With billions plundered. Flood projects called for but remain non-existent. And public accountability consistently witheld. One can only see which side of the battle you truly place yourselves on. To all my fellow [REDACTED_NAME] from Cebu, or have family and friends currently residing in Cebu, please check in with your loved ones regularly. has been a stark reminder of the uncertainty and fragility of life - you'll never know when the last conversation you'll ever have with them will be. And to all my friends and loved ones in Cebu right now who may come across this, please...please be safe. Please.

I completed an honors course in [NCR]. I now live a happy and I'd say somewhat successful life, but it is only after healing from years of self-doubt. I went to [NCR] as a scholar. Taga-probinsya. I know what my peers thought about me. That I did not deserve the scholarship because I was indeed struggling, that I was "super" probinsyana, etc. Sometimes, the disparity was just too obvious even though it was not intended. The block would have lunch outside campus and I would not join kasi ang mahal ng pagkain. Mind you, it was less than 500php, but back then, that was a lot of money for me. I could not practice programming for my coursework because I didn't own a laptop. You get the gist. I didn't feel like I belong. To top it all off, I had no support system as my parents were obviously not in Manila. While I'm happy and proud of being an [REDACTED_DEMONYM] now, I was depressed during my college years. I would walk out of classrooms because I could not breathe. I had anxiety attacks and ideations of sui*. When I graduated, I was finally able to take a deep sigh of relief. I was away from judgy eyes and ill-spoken words. It's a good thing I'm very stubborn. Despite it all, I promised to build up my confidence again. I took the chance to find myself. Why am I posting this? Because it's reunion season and memories of my old college life resurfaced. While I have healed from my insecurities, I still can't think of facing my batchmates or coursemates. I felt emotionally fatigued trying to blend in. I don't see that changing even now. So maybe if you're reading this and you have a "promdi" classmate, please be a little kinder. We all have our own battles. I'm just glad I overcame mine.

Maybe just a little call out but.. If you are not ready to pursue ANYTHING with someone na nilalandi mo, STOP. As in stop doing whatever it is you are doing with that person. If you have not made up your mind about this person, if you are confused or basta hindi pa kayo ready to be anything with the person that you like or are interested in just stop. Leading a person on is genuinely such a horrible thing to do. If you, yourself KNOW that you are not ready to fully commit or go out on dates or to talk to this person seriously, [REDACTED_NAME] nyo. Or tell the person immediately. We need to stop this cycle of texting a person 24/7, walking them home, maybe buying them flowers or food or whatever and then telling them that you won’t pursue them when they genuinely ask where does all of that lead to. People are not a game or an experiment. They are not there for your character development or whatever. Also don’t bring them to your home to spend the night with them and then tell them casual lang tayo or “I’m not ready to pursue anything with you.” That’s gross. If you want a hookup or a FUBU pumunta ka sa bumble. [REDACTED_NAME] mo sa mga kaibigan mo na gusto mo sya, mag hoholding hands kayo and whatever [REDACTED_NAME] ending is fun time [REDACTED_NAME] mo. Don’t do that. That’s wrong and disgusting. Especially when you’re both so aware that the other person likes you or you both like each other. It’s either you tell them early on or you stop. You both will end up getting hurt in the end. Don’t be an a-hole. It’s not a good look. So to the person who did this to me, magpasalamat ka na hindi kita i-naname drop. What you did was so so wrong and I hope the next person who ends up liking you won’t have to go through the same thing I did. It hurts like hell right now because I genuinely thought you liked me. Guess I was wrong. Tell the person you like or yung nilalandi nyo your intentions. Don’t play mind games with them. Every person deserves respect and love. If you actually like the person, hindi mo sila ipaglalaroan. Be good people.

DISCLAIMER:� This post isn’t meant to spread hate or make Batanes look bad. I’m writing this to raise awareness — because it’s painful to see how lightly some of my fellow Ivatans still take mental health. It’s all ‘mental health matters’ until it’s not an aesthetic, until it’s serious and uncontrollable ... WHAT KIND OF HYPOCRISY IS THIS?! The same Ivatans who claim to understand and advocate for mental health are the ones mocking it.� What’s even worse is seeing someone in a position of influence — someone who works closely with the government — adding fuel to the fire instead of setting an example. It’s disheartening to scroll through my feed and see those posts filled with mockery, even ending with “Tmang” like it’s something to laugh about. You’d think that being a father and a husband would teach a person empathy. If that were your daughter, how would you feel? Is this really how we treat our own [REDACTED_NAME]? The woman you’re ridiculing was once a tutor, an educator — a person who deserves at least some respect. And again, this post isn’t made out of hate. It’s a reminder — to think twice about how we engage on social media and how easily we add to the noise. I’m sure if it were your relative or someone close to you in the same situation, you’d be the first to defend them. But because she stands alone, this is how she’s treated? Didn’t we learn anything from [REDACTED_NAME] case? We live in such a beautiful province, yet it’s actions like these that make it so hard to live in peace and return there. Advocacy means nothing if empathy isn’t real. Your beliefs don’t make you a better person, your behavior does. “Before you speak, let your words pass through three gates: Is it true? Is it necessary? Is it kind?”

We should be careful with characterizing the complaints. People aren't complaining that the renovations happened. In fact, I imagine many were looking forward to the renovations. People are complaining about the fact that 1) these were prioritized over things that more students objectively utilize on a daily basis (with [NCR] Gym), and 2) how hostile the implementation of some of the changes are for others (Gonz Renovations). Basically, there's a contention about that notion that 'these were worth it and show that the money is going somewhere'. It's like renovations for the sake of renovations. It's pretty on-paper, and, sure, objectively "good" but it ignores many more pressing issues that students face. It's like painting over the walls without checking if there's mold or decay inside. It's pretty, but that building's gonna fall over sooner than later.

Honestly, this is a really long response and it's just how I feel personally, but the only reason I prefer the 11:59pm deadlines has something to do with the phrase "familiar hell over unfamiliar heaven". It's just what we're used to now, but I think it's ruining the lines between my rest time and work time but I don't know how to stop feeling like I'm constantly needing more time to work or learn something. I stopped caring about grades and started caring more about whether or not I'm even retaining anything from college because how is it possible that I've read so much and can't even understand what I've done in class because I'm too focused on submitting things on time or doing discussion boards. This 11:59pm deadline puts me at a constant state of feeling too "lazy" during work hours (8am-7pm) and feeling too "tense" during rest hours (7pm-8am). I feel like I'm going crazy just spending a long night on an assignment instead of being able to turn it in right before a class, and the worst part is that I'm afraid of teachers who do make assignments due before a class. I can't tell what's better for me because these late nights studying might actually put me into a coma. There is no way that this generation is living until 50 with sleep schedules that start at 2am, and constantly bright flashing screens on top of even more flashing screens for leisure. I feel there is no time or place to exist without needing to check my emails, requirements, or messages even without being part of [NCR] orgs. I feel like 11:59pm deadlines is where we started introducing the idea that students' work hours should go way beyond class time. I'm someone who doesn't even cram and still feels the need to stay up until 11pm doing work starting 5pm with breaks in between. I want to ask for change because I genuinely don't think that I can continue on being this burnt out but I also can't tell if this is just how college education is for everyone and that maybe I'm the one who's too weak to keep up. Anyways sorry for the rant, anonymous professor, but thank you for asking that---it's nice to see profs concerned for their students. If the rest of y'all in the FW feel like I don't encapsulate how you feel then I'm so sorry but don't worry, I'm not speaking on [NCR] students' behalf (behalves? I have no idea HAHAHA), I'm just sharing my opinion. Feel free to share yours!

i want to know how other scholars are able to survive the immense pressure that comes with being one in [NCR]. i am a freshman, and since high school i've always been incredibly anxious about anything school related. in university this has just intensified even further (which i didn't think was possible) and i feel like my body perceives the day to day to be life or death situations with the way i'm innately wired to worry all the time. i am not allowed to fail. i am not allowed to be tired. i am not allowed to slack off like my peers are able to. every time i go out to eat with friends i haven't seen in awhile or take a day off i feel guilty. i am scared of what i might do to myself. i am scared of the types of thoughts of escape i've been having. most importantly, i've been scared because in the past i could always push through the intense crying spells. even if i would have them every day, i could continue and return to studying or working even as i broke down. but today i just couldn't. i couldn't bring myself to anymore. and that scares me more than anything. it's not in my nature to be a negative person so i am sorry for this. i just don't have anyone to reach out to and it's hard to find similar experiences online. i cannot possibly tell the people in my life i am feeling this way because they have their own burdens too. it feels less intrusive to post it on a freedom wall, ironically, because one can choose to stop reading at any time. but a single comment would help. i would appreciate any advice anyone could give me because i don't know how much longer i can keep going.

Guys, I’m feeling really conflicted about a situation I’m currently going through, and I need advice on how to get over it or what my next course of action should be. A few months ago, I fell for this guy my friend introduced me to. We got close, talked every day, he would take me and my friends out to eat, or we would just hang out together. Eventually, I got confident enough to confess my feelings to him, and he told me he liked me back, but we didn’t have a label. For a while, everything was fine, and I was just waiting for the day he would finally ask me to be his girlfriend and make things official. But then he suddenly became distant. He started ignoring my messages and refused to see me face-to-face. We go to the same school and have similar schedules, so there were plenty of chances for us to meet. I decided to confront him, and he apologized, saying he didn’t think we could take our relationship to the next level. I decided to leave him alone because I know my worth (lol). I vented all my frustrations to my friends, cried about the situation, and told them how much it hurt me. My self-esteem really went down because of that. I mourned what could have been until I was finally okay, and I got used to not having our spontaneous outings and constant texts anymore. For a while, everything felt normal again, until my friend started acting suspicious. The same friend who introduced us stopped hanging out with us. Before, she would always join our group whenever we went out, so it was strange that she suddenly could not come, especially since she is blockmates with one of our other friends who could still join us. Meanwhile, the guy I used to talk to started posting gym stories, and that was when I began to get suspicious because my friend had just gotten a membership at that same gym. At first, I did not think much of it. To be honest, they were friends before I came into the picture, so it was normal for them to hang out. But then the guy started posting notes (yes, I did not block him) that were obviously directed toward someone, and my friend started posting pictures that looked like she was soft-launching someone. That was when all my suspicions were confirmed. I did not confront her. I just waited for her to tell me herself because part of me was hoping I was wrong, that it was all in my head, and that my friend of over five years would never do that to me. Then one day, she texted me and asked if we could call. That was when she told me everything. She was crying, apologizing, and saying she never meant to catch feelings for him. I asked if they were serious, and she told me they were not there yet but that he seemed to really like her. She even showed me their conversations, and I could clearly see the difference in how he treated her compared to me. It was obvious he liked her more, and that hurt me so much. I asked who else knew, and she told me that everyone did except me. Apparently, they had been seeing each other for a few weeks already, and our friends kept telling her to come clean before I found out on my own and got angry, so it would be better if I heard it from her. When she said that, I just felt so sad and betrayed. The fact that all our friends knew and chose to cover for her made me sick. It really made me question what real friendship even means. She asked if I was mad at her, but I told her I needed time to process everything. I ended the call feeling so conflicted. On one hand, I felt angry because she knew all about my pain, saw me cry over him, and still chose to pursue a “relationship” with him. On the other hand, she is my best friend. I consider her a sister, and I really value our friendship. I do not want to throw all that away over some guy. So that is where I need advice. Do I cut her off, or do I keep the friendship? I keep telling myself that I do not really have the right to be mad since there was never an official “us,” but honestly, would you date a guy you know broke your friend’s heart? It feels like a real-life [REDACTED_NAME] and [REDACTED_NAME] situation because I would have never done that to her or to any of my friends. - 🧚‍♀️

It's funny to me that some students are complaining that some profs didn't cancel class during the walkout. Sure, on one hand, it could be viewed as solidarity VS non-solidarity. On the other hand, it's a lesson that all decisions as an adult have consequences, and that some things (like going against corruption) are worth the sacrifice. Sometimes you're given tough options, and you just have to choose which option you can live with. Get out of your bubbles. The world won't always bend for you. If you want to stand for something, fight for it. If a cut in class is enough to deter your resolve, maybe you didn't really believe in the thing you think you wanted to fight for.

I do believe that the candidate is very competent and capable but personally, as a freshie and an activist, I think there still is a clear lack of policy focused on student welfare and representation and some of the proposed policies are already well-established by sanggu even without a president such as political education and involvement in student activism. Yes, activism is a must and should be promoted more, but it should not come at the cost of not grounding policies that help alleviate the problems that Ateneans face daily. We do not need a sanggu that will act as an echo chamber of the admin; of [REDACTED_NAME] Ang. Advanced town halls are unnecessary when the admin already hosts one directly with the students. What we need is a sanggu that will stand up to the admin and fight vocally for our rights as students. Additionally, the policies are too vague and open to interpretation. This applies to all candidates, in general. All of them share the same vague policies that have no proper execution methods and are just simple promises that will never materialize anyway. Look at the course rep candidates! They're trying to be a sanggu president with the ambitious programs they have. Mind you, they don't even have priority bills to file for the central and their respective school assemblies. They are the legislative branch of our sanggu yet they are oblivious to that fact and consistently campaign on policies that just further bloat our already bloated sanggu. I am not here to bring the candidates down, but I am here to be critical of them and to make their shortcomings known to the general voter base. It is not a requirement for us to have a sanggu president! So do not vote as if we need a sanggu president now, but vote based on merit and how the candidate will perform should they be elected. We are powerful, and we have collectively shown that power all throughout the multiple elections that have passed. So, let's not waste it over the urgency to elect an official just for the sake of electing one. And based on all the reasons stated above, I will vote to abstain. So get out and vote! Use your power to send a message to our candidates and future candidates that we need leaders who will focus on us first and our welfare. We cannot be active outside if we are inactive in the inside.

Things I wanted to say after finishing my first semester in a new country as a foreigner During our last class, my professor said, “I also learned a lot from you, even though you’re younger.” I don’t know why, but that one line stayed with me. Maybe that’s why I’m finally writing the things I was too shy to say out loud this whole semester. The start should be answering the question People kept asking me, “Why did you come to this school?” I always gave different answers— to study again, to make friends, to learn the culture. All true, but never the real reason. The truth was something I didn’t want to admit because I didn’t want to look weak. My mother passed away, and I just wanted to escape the place where it happened. I didn’t come here bravely— I came here because staying where I was hurt too much. It’s Ironic how I tried to run away from life but ended up in a place where I had to rebuild one. Thankfully, I was lucky. At least I had a friend who shared her school life stories with me, and it helped me think about something else for a while. But honestly? There wasn't such a paradise waiting just because you ran away. Studying in English for the first time was overwhelming. There were days when I couldn’t understand a single sentence in class. Being a foreigner wasn’t an excuse, also I never wanted to use it as one. But even if I struggled academically, I didn’t walk away from this semester empty-handed. When the semester began, I realized something on the very first day— that maybe this place wouldn’t be as lonely as I feared. During ORSEM, the friend who sat beside me talked to me first. Just a simple “HI,” but it calmed me down more than that person will ever know. That moment became the first small crack in the wall I built around myself. I still think about it. I’m still grateful. After that day, I tried to live differently— talking to people first, trying to joke around, joining orgs, joining sports, doing anything that looked interesting. It wasn’t smooth or natural. I had aware about it. Honestly, I probably looked awkward and insecure half the time. But it was the best I could do with who I was. And while I was trying so hard to adjust, I found myself learning a lot from the people around me. Especially my batchmates younger than me—were more mature, more hardworking, more disciplined. Watching all of you, I learned more than you think. Yes, sometimes I was jealous. Because I thought we were different. Sometimes frustrated at myself. But I kept trying. I used to be the type who never started conversations, but this semester, there were moments when I took the first step. Small moments, but meaningful ones. Then every weekend, when I went back to the place where I used to live, people told me, “You look happier. You look better than before.” That’s when I realized it: change doesn’t hit all at once dramatically. It comes quietly—slowly—one small choice at a time. Now, I don’t want to pretend anymore. I genuinely want to know the people I met here. I want to get closer. I want to build real friendships. It took me almost the whole semester to reach that point, but it’s only the first one. There’s still time. So here’s what I was too embarrassed to say: Maraming-maraming [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] (Is this right?) Thank you so much, Truly I was the only one who was worrying I’d be treated differently just because I’m from another country. But not once did I feel alone here. And one more confession: I really wanted to be a cool, funny unc this semester. I hope I wasn’t a weird or bad memory in what’s supposed to be one of the most important years of your lives. I’m writing this anonymously because I’m not ready to say these things with my real name yet. But someday, I hope I can make real friends here— people I can trust, people I can talk to, and people I can support the same way they unknowingly supported me. There were so many things I wanted to tell. Also If my failures or experiences can help someone, I’d be glad to share them someday. As for the future... I just want to be happy. I don't want to regret anymore. I want to be able to say, confidently, that coming to this school wasn’t running away— it was my choice. And it was a right choice Thanks for every people I met here. It was right As my first Filipino friend said the most valuable thing in this school was not other things It was literally people you meet in the school. As this semester ends, I hope every one of you had a good one. Merry Christmas, happy holidays, and happy New Year. See you all next semester P.S. To the professors and groupmates who had to deal with the most confused student ever. I’m so sorry, and thank you for being patient. I will and have to do better next semester. Promise.

Sorry but I am with the math department here (at least most of them), A4 paper is better than blue books. A4, I can just take out pages I don’t feel are satisfactory, and I can just replace it without taking away possible space from me. Blue books on the other hand, no, I can’t put in more pages if I end up writing something I don’t want to submit and hence cross out. Also the paper quality of blue books suck. The pages are too small, and the fact that it’s blue and lined can obscure visually what you are writing. Sure it “looks more formal” but thats extremely arbitrary for something I’d argue has a better alternative. If you’re a prof, please consider just using A4, and if you already do, thank you, you have taste.

It's so strange. When i was with you, for the long time that i was with you, you kept hurting me even when you promised to change. I dont know why i was so patient. I think its because i thought that you and i had something really special. You said so yourself. But every time i talked to you about our issues, practically begged you to change, you barely would. But i knew you left better than you started because of me. And now you have a new girl, and youre the best ive ever seen you. Quit your bad habits. You carry yourself with less anger. You got kinder. You gush about how shes the one, how what you have with her is real love. She gets to have this better version of you that i helped curate, like i built you for her. I dont want to get back together with you. Not for a second did i ever want that after you broke my heart. But i think the pain of the break up is nothing compared to the realization that you didnt like me as much as i thought i did, or as much as you convinced me you did. You didnt like me enough to change. rather, i wasn't worth changing for, and all that i wanted from you -- you gave it to her. Everything i begged from you. Everything i hoped we could be. You gave it all to her. I wish you hadn't wasted my time.

i know a typhoon in the visayas is not the main concern of a metro manila university. i know the world stops turning for no one. it would be unreasonable to suspend classes or make exceptions for just any students. i know these things rationally, but yet, why can't i stop caring about all my family and loved ones - my home - as they're being put through calamity after calamity, earthquakes and typhoons? how am i supposed to focus on assignments or chill and enjoy events, and just push away the heavy tight feeling in my chest? painful concern for my dearest friends whom i can't check up on due to signal outages, anxious worry for my loved ones whose barangay names i see on the news accompanying photos and videos of deep floods and piles of rubble? here, classes go on as usual (as they reasonably should), while back home a friend of a friend has lost their house, and a friend, MY friend, has passed away. (rest easy, my guy) i should feel lucky and relieved to be out of harm's way, shouldn't i? so why do i, who isn't directly affected by the typhoon, feel so anxious? so helpless and scared? and yet still have to show up and do everything as normal? i almost wish i'd been stranded there with my family so i'd actually be justified in being unable to focus on schoolwork. this isn't necessarily a call out to [REDACTED_NAME] because i know it's objectively not their problem. i'm just finding it so impossible to keep up and keep on while everyone i love is in danger, but not me, and i'm surrounded by people clueless to it. if you care, save any condolences you might be inclined to offer and instead find legitimate channels to donate to or help with relief from typhoon tino and the recent earthquakes. advice might help & is welcome, but what could help even more is calling for accountability on flood control projects, and not just in metro manila or luzon. is this problem to do with mental health? is it a byproduct of the education system? is this related to society's expectations/standards for productivity? yes, this may be a personal issue, but i want to wager a guess that it's also a peek into the workings of much bigger problems. i don't even know who or how many people might see this, but if freedom wall does its thing i'd like for this post to start and amplify conversations on regional disaster relief, struggles of provincial students, and other relevant social issues. open your eyes. see [REDACTED_NAME], see leyte, see negros, see cebu, see dinagat, see iloilo, see capiz, see davao, see la union, see even the places i can't list off the top of my head. these are real human people's homes and lives. maybe even friends of yours are from there. talk about us, talk about these problems, because filipino resilience can only carry us so far.

As a teacher, I think this needs to be said: The allowed absences are part of your rights as a student. They're there for situations where you feel like you can't go to school on a given day. While you're still responsible for whatever you might miss during the day, we (the faculty) cannot nor should not penalize you for utilizing said absences. If you got sick during a test day, a medical certificate is enough reason for us to allow for a make-up test. Part po ito ng rights ninyo as a student. You do not need to apologize to us for missing a class day. I appreciate those kinds of emails (they're cute in a "Oh, this is like the excuse slip during [REDACTED_NAME]" way) but it's really okay!! Don't beat yourselves up too much for getting sick or getting overwhelmed by academics. We understand!!

I know this sounds so stupid, but I'm gonna cry so hard right now after messing up my LTs and midterms in my Math courses. An Open Letter to JMC I genuinely wanted to get to know you better, not just through our chats and calls but in person. I really enjoyed our conversations as they felt real and full of connection. For a while, I honestly thought we were on the same page. I was looking forward to meeting you, but then you just disappeared without a word just a few days before our planned meet-up. I wish you had told me why. Did I cross a line when I found out about you on IG? Were you hiding something important from me? Was I too demanding? Or maybe that was your intention from the start and I was just naive enough to believe otherwise. I already know some of your lies, like your age, but I also know that some of the things you told me were true. Still, there are stories I’ll probably never know if I should believe. And maybe I shouldn’t be this affected, since we only talked for a month. Still, it’s strange how something brief can leave a mark when it felt genuine at the time. I just wish you had been honest enough to tell me the truth. PS: I’m sharing this here not to call you out but as a way to process my thoughts and move forward. - NA

My dad and I were having an argument over text, and it peaked when he told me: “YOU OWE YOUR LIFE TO YOUR PARENTS!!!” This statement right here summarizes the toxic Filipino culture we live in, and it's something that has made me distrust the systems we operate in. I'm 25, I work from home, and I'm being told that I have to "give back" to a family that has invalidated my emotions, that has physically and emotionally abused me, and that has hindered my development as a person. Losing confidence and self-esteem because of bullies in high school is one thing — but when it comes from your own family? That's a different story. I'm thankful that I've met the friends, peers, and communities that helped me rediscover myself. They’ve shown me that I shouldn’t let the words of others define who I am. It's what allows me to grow detached to this kind of verbal abuse. At the same time, it angers me that this kind of culture is normalized. It breeds fear instead of respect, guilt instead of gratitude. It raises generations of people who confuse obedience with love, and silence with peace. It turns relationships into transactions — where affection has to be earned, and individuality is treated as rebellion. It seeps into our schools, workplaces, and even friendships, teaching us that pleasing others is safer than being honest. I'm hoping the admins post this because this has been something I've been sitting with for years, and I thought I'd finally address it. Parents want their kids to show undying loyalty, but they don’t want to do the work of being emotionally present, self-sufficient, or accountable for their own healing. You don’t owe your life to anyone. You owe it to yourself to live it well, and you owe it to God for allowing you to do so.

Had things gone typically or how I would have liked, I would already have graduated by now, maybe even twice. But right now, I'm still a bit far from that goal. My poor mental and physical health has put me in a position wherein I've had to be delayed by a few years. These past couple of years I have been endlessly happy to see the people I was freshies with and the batches after that graduate, and yet my head and heart ache with every congratulations I give. Whenever someone assumes that I've graduated and tries to congratulate me or ask what I graduated with, I've had to stop myself from sobbing. I don't know what I would do if I get one of my batchmates as professor. When I look around the room in my majors, I sometimes ask myself if anyone there even knows my name. At the very least, I know I'm nobody's first pick for a group, or second, or third, or fourth, or fifth. Who can blame them? I mostly keep to myself, and because my classes are from various year levels, I'm not ingrained in any year for my course. Oh well

My most cherished TOTGA story: It was sem break and there were no students in campus for obvious reasons. But me and my [REDACTED_NAME] who I’ve had a huge crush on (and definitely developed to something more later on) decided to meet in school for an important org work. While Facebook was already a huge thing then, text messaging was still the primary mode of comms. We were already both in school that day, but we couldn’t find each other because the network service was spotty for some reason. My feet brought me to the side of the MVP building, and the most fairytale-like thing happened: I was texting and as I reached the bottom of the staircase shown in the picture, I looked up, and saw her at the top of the staircase...she was texting, looked up, and saw me as well. We both smiled. And that moment was instantly saved to my memory forever. While those stairs are just stairs for many, it became the most serendipitous location on campus for me. There weren’t too many steps, but it was the slowest walk I ever had on those stairs as we met halfway, and went up the staircase together. Two people looking for each other, and ending up on opposite ends of the staircase at the exact same time. That imagery still exists vividly in my head today. While I didn’t end up marrying her (I’m married to my wife who I absolutely consider as the love of my life), I’m still happy I was able to have that core memory with my TOTGA. The side staircase of the MVP building holds a very special place in my heart, and that moment keeps replaying in my head each time I visit [NCR] and pass by that area. It’s my very own stairway to heaven indeed.

[REDACTED_NAME] mapost hehe Is cursing the new norm? As far as I know, curse words were sort of "taboo" to say before, especially in front of a child, that elderly people would often scold others when they do say a curse word, either intentionally or not. And I believe that it is to prevent children to normalize such words as it could hurt not only the child but also the parent's reputation. I would often hear words like "Sino ba nagpalaki sa batang yan?" when a child says a curse word, which is concerning for me since one of my cousins started to develop and normalize such words because he thought that it was funny or normal to say. Today, I've been curious and intrigued, "Why are curse words now being used, and even normalized, in day-to-day discussions?". Honestly, it turns me off when I hear the crunchiest p*t* words, and their english versions like there's no tomorrow. But I oftentimes hide my disapproval when I hear such words, especially among friends because I tried to fit in minus the curse words. But what's concerning for me now is that these "normal" curse words are starting to creep up to my vocabulary. Oftentimes, I'd say a curse word unconsciously, not even in face-to-face but also online. I did not want this to happen, and it sucks for me that cursing is starting to be the new norm, not only in usual talking but also in online. I believe that it shouldnt become a habit for everyone to do since I wanted an actual decent and respectful conversation. It might be okay for you to accept this new "norm", but for others, it is not. I don't know who needs to hear this right now, but please for your own sake DO NOT overglorify overworking yourself to the bone. It could be organizations, extracurriculars, or anything that is non-academic - we should also realize that there's more to university life beyond work. I've seen a lot of my friends fall into this trap (myself included) and now we're all burned out in our junior/senior year. What we got from it? Very surface-level connections, some friendships here and there, and some trauma (org-dependent)! Eitherway, we kind of found out that we're extremely replaceable with the work we do (microcosm of philippine society?!) and it's kind of sad that my seniors were just nice to me to have us stay longer for our manpower rather than out of pure empathy. They don't even keep in touch with us anymore, previously they messaged and reacted to my shared posts like a rabid twitter fan, now they barely even care to check up on the people who left. I generally thought doing a lot meant that I was heading somewhere, but now ever since leaving most of my orgs I feel more lost than ever, and it feels like I'm starting from a blank slate again similar to what I entered [REDACTED_NAME] with. I think being a workaholic is okay, but there are simply boundaries that shouldn't be crossed that would mean sacrificing your health to please the people around you. I feel like someone needs to hear this, but please know when to step back, leave, and rediscover yourself. Some, if not most org cultures feel extremely transactional and cliquish nowadays that even home orgs don't feel welcoming anymore. To the BS Org kiddies: I was also disillusioned by org life that it would allow me to meet a lot of people. I hate to say it, but people will forget you once they graduate. Focus on building more deeper connections with people from one or two orgs and go from there. And please, do not make orgs your entire personality during RecWeek season and your 20+ DPs dzai nagiging pet peeve [REDACTED_NAME] unti-unti

A VERY HOT TAKE (or maybe not.. idk) [NCR] is very much for freedom of speech and of the press. Under no circumstances, will Ateneans ever stand complicit whenever the rights of media organizations to report and investigate are trampled upon. But like, these same mainstream media organizations are also the ones consistently f*cking us (the Filipino people) over time and time again. This congressman from dasma is really ticking me off with his antics. The man has no record of graduating from any educational institution, no landmark bills or legislation, a benefactor of a political establishment, and a weirdo cat connoisseur. (Look, I have no problem with cat / animal advocates at all, but c’mon you could’ve just put up an NGO, man) Normally, these are the kind of people you ignore. But for some reasons, many of our outlets think that this man is newsworthy. Can you people seriously not figure out [REDACTED_NAME] pansin [REDACTED_NAME]? Seriously, this guy has been making noise since the flood control inquiry began, but what has he contributed thus far? Nothing. Just noise, and conspiracy theories that are unsubstantiated by his own breath and saliva. But forget all of that, his meows and kuting impressions are good for the viewership eh no? This is not the first time the media made a foolish use of their power as messengers. I also blame these people for the rise of Duterte. Even before , Duterte has already been making narrations that are telling of his morals [REDACTED_NAME]. But what did the media do? Sure, some took an investigative stance. But the vast majority, especially those with the power to broadcast treated him as a feature subject. Isa itong [REDACTED_NAME] inaming may binaril [REDACTED_NAME] campus habang siya ay isang mag-aaral. Isa itong [REDACTED_NAME] inaming [REDACTED_NAME] sa karapatang pantao. Isa itong tao na hindi itinago ang kanyang pagkahayok sa haplos [REDACTED_NAME]. But what did the media do? [REDACTED_NAME] pa ng plataporma. [REDACTED_NAME], inimbita pa sa late night show niya at tinanong tungkol sa sari-sari niyang mga girlfriends. And for what? [REDACTED_NAME]. It’s easy and convenient to blame the zealots for everything wrong. But the media cannot completely wash their hands clean of the stain of absurdity that our country is in right now, because they allowed people with f*cked up morals to have a platform. Let them be reminded, that as much as we are for their empowerment as messengers, they, too, can and should be held accountable for the gross misuse of their right to report.

Girls: “I like nerdy guys 🥺💗” Also girls: “But not... like... actual nerds. Here’s what we REALLY mean.” 1. Must wear glasses — but only the Attractive Anime Protagonist kind Not the thick, round “I program [REDACTED_NAME] for fun” glasses. You need the perfect frames that scream: “I read books... but also look hot doing it.” If your glasses make you look like you manage Excel sheets for a living → nope. 2. Must be a nerd... who looks better when the glasses come off Girls say: “I love when nerdy guys take off their glasses.” But if you actually take yours off and your face suddenly drops its Wi-Fi signal? Instant downgrade. Only pre-approved handsome nerds pass this level. 3. Must be ‘nerdy but ripped’ They want a “study boyfriend,” not a “studying destroyed my spine” boyfriend. He codes for 6 hours, then casually lifts 80kg while discussing Star Wars lore. If your body screams “final boss of math club,” they’re out. They want: “I solve equations AND have shoulders.” 4. Must study well but not in the ‘calculator in pocket protector’ way You need to carry a book so people know you’re smart... But not THAT smart. Like: “I read classical philosophy.” Not: “I read classical philosophy for fun.” 5. Must have little dating experience—but still act smooth Girls: “I want a nerd who’s shy 🥺” Also girls: “But he must be confidently shy??” If your voice cracks like a broken Bluetooth speaker when she says hi → rejected. They want the guy who says he’s never dated but somehow flirts like he has DLC unlocked. 6. Must dress like a nerd... but a Pinterest nerd Hoodies okay. Cardigans okay. But not the “my mom bought this for 50% off at a hiking store” kind. They want the guy who looks like a Korean drama librarian, not “lost his way to calculus class.” 7. Must be clean despite being a ‘nerd’ You can study 10 hours a day... but also smell like fresh laundry. If you look like you haven’t seen sunlight since midterms → OUT. Conclusion: Girls don’t actually want nerds. They want “a handsome, fit, stylish, confident, clean man who occasionally reads.”

[REDACTED_NAME] sungit [REDACTED_NAME], yung [NCR] traffic IS THE WORSE I'VE EXPERIENCED SO FAR (even inside [NCR] grabe inabot [REDACTED_NAME] wait [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] grab inside the campus! Take note, it was raining today: pagkalabas namin [REDACTED_NAME] (THERE IS NO USABLE CHAIR WHATSOEVER dahil basa+dirty) and we have to wait for 2 hrs outside. [REDACTED_NAME] inconvenient. Even the grab driver told us [REDACTED_NAME] daw nagiging rason bakit [REDACTED_NAME]. How's the traffic management here?! And to be frank I can't help but compare my experience in [NCR]. 1. [NCR] staff and guards are accomodating and VERY welcoming. They would welcome goodmorning and kahit outsiders they are very warm. In [NCR], knowing na it's raining—we are guests for an event tapos basa yung chairs outside. [REDACTED_NAME] man [REDACTED_NAME] offer for us to go inside and wait (apparently the event is over and mahigpit sila for outsiders but I hope they could just smile and apologise for the inconvenience? In [NCR] we have an airconditioned waiting area, even charging outlets for outsiders. Guards would even ask how we are or if our sundo has arrived + if raining, they'd even initiate to chaffeur us since they don't want us [REDACTED_NAME]. [NCR], in my experience, has: ✅ Warmer and more accommodating guards/staff ✅ Better and more accessible facilities ✅ More convenient campus surroundings — cafés, transport, and walking areas that don’t sabotage your heels when it rains. Meanwhile, [NCR] definitely has strong academic reputation and a beautiful, nature-rich campus... but daily convenience? Traffic? Pavements that turn into rainfall obstacle courses? There’s room to grow. Every school has its strengths naman but as a graduate student juggling many responsibilities, comfort, accessibility, and overall environment matter just as much as academics. This experience made me appreciate how thoughtfully designed [NCR] is for working students. A great education includes kindness, design, and empathy. Top Universities should also make sure its students and staff/professors feel seen, safe, and supported in every corner of the campus.

Thoughts on “know-it-alls”? I keep getting grouped with this person who’s smart and hardworking, sure, but the dominance is unreal. They do the “I’m listening” act, then twist everyone’s ideas until they’re basically theirs. Disagree and they’ll push nonstop until you’re too tired to argue. Meetings? Always on their schedule. If someone can’t make it, they shrug and say it’s fine to skip instead of adjusting. After tasks are assigned, they rewrite people’s parts without telling anyone, like our time and effort don’t matter. And because they’re a social butterfly everyone seems to love, the downside gets ignored. The “good work ethic” masks control issues—maybe that’s what happens when you live in one school bubble your whole life.

I feel stupid, ugly, and lazy.. I feel like I do not even deserve God himself [REDACTED_NAME]. I want to get help.. but who can help someone who can't help themselves. She's going to catch me. And, I want her to do it. So, turns out my blockmates found my previous post ( ). The one where I professed my love for K from AB Lit. She still doesn't know who I am, and neither do my blockmates (and they probably never will know). And, that is part of the problem. The thing is... I'm scared of being judged. I know I'm not enough. I know that she's way out of my league. Plus, my blockmates found my message weird and creepy, talking about how I want to snuggle my face in her hair or have a family and raise six cats. Like, why are they even judging me? It's not like it's abnormal to have these thoughts. I'm sure everyone has had them at least once in their lifetime, especially all of you wattpad readers (no judgement). Anyways, my point still stands. I am afraid to be open with her. I am afraid to pursue her. And most of all, I am afraid of rejection. It's been months of talking and getting to know each other during our classes. Every time we get grouped together for a project, I thank the universe. Every time she smiles at me, or even looks my way, I can't help but feel like an angel had just dawned upon me. The air around her is radiant, it's divine. SHE is divine. And still, I don't think I have it in me to ever muster up the courage to confront her about these feelings and it is killing me inside. Sometimes, I wish that she liked me back. Maybe somehow, she feels for me too. I know it's just wishful thinking. It's probably just one-sided, unrequited love. But, I can't help but cling to the possibility that maybe she gets the same butterflies that I do whenever she looks at me. So K from AB Lit, if you're reading this, I hope you'll catch me in the act soon. I hope you'll see through my facade, and break down my walls. I hope that we can be together, despite the judgement. I have never felt like this for anyone before and just know that everything I said is genuine, written from the heart just like Ma'am [REDACTED_NAME] taught us in [REDACTED_NAME] (even though I know your opinions about her). I say this like a prayer. I need you in my life, so I can finally stop dreaming about you and make you my reality. I'm a coward, [REDACTED_NAME] But, I'm a coward for you, and I hope you'll see the person underneath all of that fear one day.

Hi! I’m not from [NCR] but I did write this for someone in [NCR] The color blue has a lot of representation. Blue can mean sadness, blue can mean calmness, or blue can mean trust. I used to think blue was suppose to represent sadness, l used to watch inside out and I saw that sadness was color blue, so I soon adapted in my mind that blue was sadness. One day, as I doom scroll through facebook, I saw a familiar face, a face that I could never forget, a face I used to admire for hours on end. HE was wearing blue, for a moment, I froze, expecting that familiar sting of sadness. But instead, something was different, the blue did not look lonely and sad. It looked peaceful, gentle, hopeful, even comforting. Seeing him in that color made me realize that blue wasn't always about sorrow- it also meant serenity, depth, quiet strength, and HOME. In that moment, it felt like the color had changed meaning right before my eyes. Maybe it wasn't the color blue that had changed-maybe it was me.

Dear Lasallians, When I see you — all charm and chaos, with that smug grin that smells of privilege and power — I feel both fury and fascination coil in my chest. Because at least we, [REDACTED_NAME], were taught to seek meaning — to think before we strike, to conquer with conscience. But Lasallians? You shoot first and call it passion. You mistake recklessness for courage, confidence for character. You think the world is yours to win, to own, to play. And yet there you are, pointing your gun of wit and arrogance at me. “You are the shooter,” you whisper, “and I am the prey.” But no, you’re wrong. You may try to shoot me down, but you will always miss. Because I am not your prey — I am your mirror. The reflection you refuse to face. The calm after your riot. The thought behind your impulse. You burn in neon green — loud, alive, and shameless. I bleed in [NCR] blue — quiet, deliberate, and proud. You dance through chaos; I breathe through discipline. You play to win; I play to transcend. You chase thrill; I chase truth. And yet, somewhere between our dueling colors, something dangerous hums — a fascination that tastes like sin. Maybe that’s why we keep circling each other. The shooter and the prey. The hunter and the heir. Because what we hate most about each other is what we envy most. You, with your freedom. Me, with my restraint. You, born to break the world. Me, born to build it. Together, we are contradiction — fire and intellect, recklessness and reason. But in the end, we were never meant to meet halfway. For while you aim for the world, I aim beyond it — and an Archer will never touch the sky meant only for an Eagle.

This year’s one big pride especially the opening night was disappointing. It was a flop 🫤 They really chose a field for a venue knowing that it’s rainy season, so everyone there got muddy feet and it the whole event pretty much became a slipping hazard to everyone. Covered court is there oh, perfect venue —- covered from rain, solid ground, still malawak Not to mention the booths are kinda underwhelming. Volunteer-led booths are underequipped (I like the face painting booth and the merchs were pretty cool tho) and food stalls are kinda eh. Not much freebies either unlike last year’s opening night Technical difficulties which pretty much set back the whole program by almost an hour. I’m writing this kasi, honestly, I’m super disappointed. I really thought ASOG was supposed to be the training ground for ethical, competent, and reform-minded public servants. [REDACTED_NAME] lately, it feels like we’re tolerating the exact opposite. Let’s be real: most students are openly using AI or sharing answers during exams, and it’s just brushed off. Like, how does that even test our grasp of governance theory or ethical leadership? That’s basically just a Google speed test. And yes, I get that some profs are super strict and uphold high standards (props to them, talaga). But the inconsistency is wild. Some students literally brag about getting away with shortcuts and that’s such a bad look for a school that preaches integrity and service. It’s giving hypocrisy, not heroism. We deserve better. ASOG deserves better. [REDACTED_NAME] if we keep coddling cheaters and pretending it’s fine, we’re not producing servant-leaders; we’re just recycling the same kind of officials we’re all supposedly trying to replace.

But truthfully, there’s something about you that’s hard to ignore. You have this presence — light, calm, and magnetic — that just makes everything feel a bit easier, a bit brighter. You don’t even try, but people naturally gravitate toward you. It’s been a while since I last saw you, now it’s mostly through online meetings. But every time your name appears on screen or I hear you speak, it somehow makes the class feel lighter. Like even law feels less heavy when you’re there. I still remember when you told us, “Padayon.” You said it means keep going. I hope you tell yourself that too, especially on the days you forget how inspiring you actually are. Future Atty, keep going. With that same warmth, confidence, and quiet strength that sets you apart. I hope you’re treated well and that no one ever tries to take away that genuine happiness in you. The world honestly feels brighter with you in it. And if I can be real for a moment, you’re beautiful, [REDACTED_NAME]. Not just in the way people usually mean it, but in that rare, effortless kind of way that stays. Maybe it’s your soul, maybe it’s your drive, or maybe it’s just you. Thank you for being the reason I keep pushing to become an attorney someday. Respectfully,� Someone who quietly admires you, maybe a little more than I should 😉

helloo just wanted to give a PSA to everyone who tambays in starbs along [NCR], I know how nice it is to stay there for long hours to study and all but please share your charging sockets! I was on sat on the couches by the right side when you enter starbs (the one beside shakeys). I sat beside this girl and the socket was in between us. TL;DR in the broader perspective of things learn to share, you ain’t kinder no more

something i noticed in the [NCR] community is that GENERALLY, many of us live good and comfortable lives. most of us have all the basic needs, we strive and achieve success, and have capabilities to buy or experience many things. i had everything that i could want or ever ask for, but for some reason there’s this emptiness in my heart that i cannot explain. an emptiness that eats in my chest. man it was so painful and i was hopeless. no one knew. i tried filling it with more success, more validation, more things, but that void in my heart grew and grew. i searched for answers trying to find peace, love, and hope that i sooo longed for. because i felt that i always had to prove myself and earn love my whole life. i never believed in depression but it got so bad that i wanted to off myself. my life was falling apart - my passions, my family, my relationship, and myself. yet in the moment that would be my last, i found the solution or rather, the solution found me. i was overwhelmed with love, peace, joy, and hope that i never ever could find or receive. i didnt want my life so i gave it to someone who did... [REDACTED_NAME]. He loves you so much and wants only a relationship with you. He’s not asking you to be perfect or follow all the rules, He just wants to know you. we don’t need to earn His love because we never had to work for it in the first place. His love is a gift, which is freely given by Him. He has a reserved seat for you in heaven because He’s paid our debt of sin, in full, once and for all. that’s why He said “it is finished”. if this message finds you, God wants you to know that He’s reaching out. He’s transformed my life - because of Him, my heart is sooo full. full of love, peace, joy, hope, and purpose. i wouldnt be here sharing this message if it wasn’t for Him who saved me. hebrews 10:14-17 🫶

[NCR] could take a more reflective approach in assessing whether the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) it upholds—particularly SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) and SDG 13 (Climate Action)—are truly being implemented in practice. For example, many classrooms rely on multiple ceiling fans, stand fans, and even several old air conditioning units just to maintain barely a comfortable temperature. This setup seems energy-inefficient and counterproductive to sustainability goals. Moreover the student’s well being! It seems as though students are more focused on NOT passing out rather than listening to the teacher. Hygiene, personal well being, and the conduciveness of learning is at stake! While simply opening windows or doors may have worked in the past , it becomes impractical during heavy rain or extreme heat. Given the urgency of climate change, investing in renewable energy solutions like solar panels could be a more sustainable and forward-thinking move. Though the initial cost is high, the long-term benefits include significant savings on electricity expenses, which could then be redirected toward other campus improvements, like upgrading the covered courts or East Parking area.! Or maybe patching up the roof of JSEC so it stops dripping while im just trying to eat my food! Please!

It kind of saddens me how it's become so normal for orgs to be completely beginner unfriendly if they become popular enough. Imagine coming into an org thinking that you found a community centered around the thing you've always wanted to learn only to be faced with auditions, trials, dares, rigorous interviews and the like just to even be CONSIDERED for the org. It really promotes a very depressing mentality of not being worth anything if you didn't have the foresight years ago to train yourself on a specific skill. It's very much the opposite of the whole point of going to college, where we're here to LEARN from others more experienced than us, not go here to flex our skills to everyone. To all the orgs out there, I'm begging you, please be kinder to beginners. You never know, maybe one of them really is a prodigy in the making, they just never had the space to learn until now. Even if they don't end up being a prodigy, don't burn out genuine passion just because you don't find them worth your time.

I feel lost and trapped, I’m currently disabled and unable to walk as much. I’m going through therapy to overcome my condition, but it pains me that others just think I’m just playing the fool and pretending. It’s not like I wanted to be disabled, I want to walk just as much as the next person. It feels like other people don’t understand how serious my condition is and I’m just wailing my concerns and they see it as me deflecting. My dad got so angry that he took my crutches away, he can’t understand my predicament that I still can’t walk. I’m trying so hard in therapy to be able to progress. He doesn’t believe in it even after I’ve been seeing a Doctor that says my condition is severe and we’re trying to progress through therapy. He doesn’t see my efforts, he got so angry because I tried to defend myself by speaking up about what I was feeling but somehow they always see it as “talking back.” He threw things at me and it hit me, and he’s never hit me that bad before. I know he’s tired, I know it, but as much as it pains me to hear it from others that I’m not trying hard enough, I have to hear the same degrading words from him. I know my own progression and we’re slowly reaching our goals, my end goal is to be able to walk again he doesn’t believe in my therapy. For him the small progressions I’ve had aren’t that big, he doesn’t see the pain I go through just to be able to advance in though every exercise. When he told me to get out of the house and leave that he doesn’t want to see me at home, I’m lost I don’t know what to do.

just a reminder to everyone: asking your profs to move your classes online due to a transport strike is STRIKE BREAKING. you are not standing in solidarity with the strike, you are actively helping break their strike for your own personal gain and convenience. "but some students rely on the public transportation to get to class" thats the POINT. the laborers in the transport strike are essential parts of our society that have to be valued and given just compensation, and the inconvenience that the public faces by their strike is the main point of their movement. this is the very same reason as to why asking your profs not to count cuts during a walk out defeats the purpose of a walk out. so the next time their is a transport strike or an [NCR] labor union strike, i implore you to stand in solidarity with the unions and DO NOT ASK TO CANCEL OR MOVE CLASSES ONLINE! the inconvenience we feel is the sacrifice we make in respect of the rights of laborers. and if you can, join their strike as well. the sacrifice we make to go to class is nothing compared to the sacrifices that the laborers make every single day to ensure that our country keeps moving.

But it is almost perfect if we choose to look at the all things it has to offer that we should be grateful for. Why does this keep on happening? Am I disrupting a time sequence because I'm writing this right now? I feel like I’m the disposable and optional friend. Hey, there. To be honest, I don’t know what’s wrong with me either. I’m not too much of an introvert, yet I’m not that loud either. Not dumb but not that smart either. Has some value, but I think it’s not that memorable. Ever since before college, it’s the cycle I’ve noticed. I gain friends whom I will care about deeply, and then when time goes by I’ll notice that I’m the only one without a stable friend to go by. I was fine in any circle of friends (as long as we’re in the same vibes); I find an advantage to that. However, it won’t be long until they’ll leave me behind. It won’t be long until I’ll hear stuff like how I annoy them either. It always left me wondering where I went wrong. I self-monitor myself everytime, I ensure that I don’t overstep boundaries too hard or not really at all (depends on my perceived closeness with them, so I guess that’s a flaw). I ensure to share my stuff (notes, flashcards, books) with everyone else. I vibe with them. I don’t speak ill of people to the point that I want something bad to happen to them. I know to separate someone from their work ethic. I know how to be objective and still care—so why? Why do people always seem to leave me? Do I truly annoy them? How? How should I not annoy them, then? I try to be useful in every way I can; yet why is it not always enough? I’m in second year now, and I just feel stupid for even believing that I might find friends who will need me as a friend rather than what I can do for them. I thought it’s them, and while I understand our differing schedules and such, I feel so ashamed and hurt and just confused why I’m the only one who feels distant and lonely, and the rest of my friendgroup remain close as ever—maybe even closer. They even share inside jokes that I don’t know and just feel tired to explain to me so I just read through the lines and get it myself. I know they still care for me as I do for them, but I yearn for what was before; yet maybe it’s just me who thought that, felt that, and missed that. There’s this one friend from that friend group whom I miss. An org-oriented person whom I fully support until now. I miss them so much. However I just noticed how cold they are to me now, making me realize and experience (once more, in) full blast how painful it is for a close friend to suddenly become a stranger again. Maybe it’s just my demons whispering to me, but I notice everytime they don’t respond to my rare chats, how they not react to my chats in the groupchats, how they not reply to me in particular, so on and so forth. My fear came back, and it’s just making me think just what did I do to not be enough for this person anymore? I’m not angry at my friends, even if the term is one-sided now. I loved and love them with all my heart, yet I just wish that I am not that disposable and optional friend. I always speak of everyone fondly, but I wonder if they do the same to me. Am I special to them like they are to me, or am I just someone there? Worse, was I just nobody? I hope I gain more bonds, honestly, but my anxiety of not being enough will probably eat me up again. Fighting, self... Thank you for reading through this. Honestly, I think I need therapy. I don’t know how to do stuff related to the OGC, and I’m quite afraid of the price, haha.

long post ahead but here we go: tldr: i'm drained, burnt out maybe. i don't feel like myself before my college life started. is it just me or is this sem so tiring? it should be since it's finals already but, i feel so ABNORMALLY tired. i feel less motivated to go to class because i feel like i don't need to since i have other work to do but when faced with work i hold it off til the day of the deadline. i did not do this before. in senior high, i'd work days before the deadline. i keep wondering why is my motivation degrading? was i too hard on myself? am i being too lenient with myself? i was probably too hard on myself in the first sem bc probably i wanted ppl to like me and have a good impression of me. but now, maybe im being too lenient and "let myself go" because it's so hard to keep up this quality of work. but bc of this, i think ive disappointed numerous ppl...maybe some ppl hate me alr and im dreading the next few weeks bc ill be getting just a few weeks of vacation until intersesssion. i usually take the advice "dont be too hard yourself esp in college" to heart, but do you think i took it too far? nowadays i just want to stay at home and sleep for hours. i still contribute to work but not at my best. what happened to me? what did i do to myself? i hate myself even more now. i want to disappear forever. this was my fault. i chose the school. i chose the course. i made all these decisions. there's no one else to blame but myself. i still like this school and ive had great experiences, [REDACTED_NAME] i think i need like 10 years to recover. no, ill be generous. maybe a whole month to recover. that's all, thank you.

(maybe hot take) while i do understand the frustration of many ashs students, it feels invalidating when they claim to be entitled to an admission slot more than others just because they were already in the institution since hs (or just bc they were already scholars in shs). ik there are several ashs students who more than deserving to be in [NCR]. but i don't think they understand that every applicant was considered by the oaa fairly. it's not like being in ashs is going to give you a leg up. i dont think they understand how broader the application pool is for [NCR], compared to ashs (including in scholarship applications). i also saw others claim how schools like ust or [NCR] are more considerate towards their shs students, but doesnt that beat the point of college admissions in the first place? people should pass admissions because they passed, and not because they were (academically or financially) privileged to invest in 2 extra years of [NCR] shs education. maybe you were eligible for finaid then, but that status isn't assured now, given that there are more people (ig from provinces, public schools) that could be more in need/more academically deserving. i just don't get the entitlement i've read from some ashs submissions here. all your hardwork is valid, and maybe that frustration is valid, but that doesn't mean other people don't deserve what you didn't get.

honestly, some beadles forget what their role is supposed to be. the job is to help communicate and make things easier for the class — not to complicate what’s already clear. when the local government already announces a suspension, there’s no need for extra polls or “clarifications” that only cause more confusion. what makes it worse is when the tone in the gc turns dismissive or condescending. people are just asking questions or reacting normally, yet they get responses that feel unnecessarily rude. it doesn’t build respect, it just creates resentment. a reminder lang: leadership isn’t about asserting authority for the sake of it. it’s about being approachable, considerate, and actually helpful to the people you’re supposed to represent. anything less just makes you unlikable.

Heads up, [REDACTED_NAME]... What if I told you that the “cute mutual” you’ve been flirting with on IG or Twitter might not just be fake, but also out to ruin your life? Recently, more and more cases have surfaced of Ateneans-- yes, actual people you sit beside in class or pass by in Zen, getting baited into s*xting or video calls by people pretending to be pretty girls or guys. These scammers are good. Too good. They curate accounts so well you’d never suspect they’re catfish. They’ll flirt, rush things, then push you into a VC where they either hire women to show explicit stuff or use prerecorded clips. Meanwhile, they’re recording you. Those recordings don’t stay private. They end up on X, Telegram, or worse-- sold for profit. And yes, athletes, org kids, and just regular students are being targeted. This matters because the victims aren’t some random strangers on the internet. They’re classmates, teammates, and friends. Many are still in college, and some of these cases involve people we actually see on campus. Even if you’ve met someone in person before, the threat doesn’t disappear-- recordings can happen anytime. And because they know [REDACTED_DEMONYM] circles so well, it suggests these people could be students, alumni, or individuals closely tied to our community. That’s why it’s important to stay mindful. If someone you’re talking to moves too fast, pushes for intimacy without trust, or avoids meeting you in safe, real spaces, that’s a red flag. Don’t let your guard down just because the account looks legit. Protect yourself. Think twice before hopping on a VC with someone you don’t really know. This isn’t to scare anyone, but to remind you that your reputation, safety, and dignity are worth far more than a few minutes of thrill.

i am an econ major, and as much as i “like” my professors as people the quality of teaching is admittedly really low. but it doesnt even end there, its the curic itself - why was ECON 110 removed? I firmly believe that we should have put in first year and not remove it, replace it with one major, probably MATH 21. Why? because it allows profs to go deeper in ECON 111 and 112, and second, it is best that econ students have an early experience with econ if they actually like it. - ECON 111 has become the new 110. It is disappointing that very few profs truly fulfill the clo of the use of calculus in micro. Whereas other universities have much more in depth classes due to a wider toolbox of mathematical techniques. - ECON 115/6 (Stat and MathEcon) These courses need to be reworked. They are either repeats of highschool/early college math classes for the first half. And the teaching for these classes are honestly terrible, shoutout to L.L. - ECON 117 is now half baked. Due to AB EC being unable to understand matrix algebra they suffer in the course. Have mathecon a bit earlier! (no, AB EC should not take MATH 40.1). Sorry, but we are truly falling behind. Dear Econ Department: Teach better, care more about the academics (AND NOT JUST INTERNSHIPS), and have more depth in your courses, i assure that the students are capable to handle more difficult coursework given enough support! Do better.

I’m honestly not sure if posting this here is a good idea, but this has been bothering me and I just need to put it out there. So, I live in the dorms on campus. Since there aren’t many decent study spaces open late (and no, the mosquito-ridden dorm study spaces don’t count unless you’re okay with dengue as a study buddy), I usually end up at cafes along Katip. The problem is, I lose track of time and often end up staying past curfew, which is at 12 midnight. It’s not a big deal because I can still enter the campus, but it means I have to walk from Gate 3 to UD—which is more than a kilometer. In complete darkness. [NCR] likes to save electricity by turning off all the street lamps after curfew. At first, I didn’t mind. The midnight walks were actually kind of nice—quiet, breezy, peaceful. Sometimes I’d have friends with me, but most of the time it’s just me making my way back. That’s when I started noticing... him. There’s this tall, lanky guy I’ve seen multiple times now, always near the St. [REDACTED_NAME] More statue by the Old Lib. Round glasses, thick hair parted in the middle. He’s usually walking the opposite direction, toward Gate 3. I’ve probably crossed paths with him six or seven times. We never talk, never even acknowledge each other, but every single time I come across him, he slows down, turns slightly toward me like he’s about to say something. but never does. Very very sus. Here’s where it gets creepier. I’ve never really seen his face clearly, but sometimes the moonlight reflects off his glasses so I am aware that at least he has a face. I haven’t tried to catch his eyes (because stranger danger!) but there’s this ominous mood that come from him. The other thing is his keys. I always hear them jingling in his pocket as soon as I pass SocSci. I’ll hear them until he’s passed me by. At first I didn’t think much of it, but then I realized, every time we do cross paths, the sound stops after just a few steps. I hadn’t ever tried to look back to check on him in the past but just tonight, he was once again right there, almost on cue, walking past St. [REDACTED_NAME] More in the dark. I kept an ear out for the same keys that been creeping me out. I see him walking, the keys are jingling... and then silence. I turned around to see where he went. But he was gone. No one was there. I got spooked out and basically half-ran back to the dorm. Luckily my roommates were still awake, so I told them the whole thing. None of them have ever seen this guy even if some of them go past curfew as well. So now I don’t know if I’m losing it, if my sleep-deprived brain is just playing tricks on me from all the late nights, or if I’ve been walking past something I really don’t want to know about. Either way, I’m not risking it anymore. I’m so not breaking curfew again.

Hot take: [REDACTED_NAME]'s direction to shift online due to class suspensions from inclement weather is probably correct. With one condition: I do not think cuts should be counted during synchronous online sessions. Though then again, students can always (and do) contact their professors in case they have issues with connectivity. In most cases, considerations are made for those who cannot attend as well. I do hope everyone talks to their prof. I personally live in a flood prone area and my profs have always been considerate in the rare instance that this takes out my internet connection. Of course, it takes a certain privilege to be able to attend synchronously during inclement weather, but we are talking about one of the most privileged universities in the country here. The average student is privileged. Learning has to continue. Needless to say, none of this applies in cases of particularly severe or life-threatening rains, but the average DILG suspension suspends classes due to even average storms (preventative in nature, even if the rains turn out to be not severe at all, the way it should be). My family is struggling to pay 15k per 3-unit subject here. I would rather push through with learning than feel like my semester is incredibly shortened due to the lack of synchronous sessions, especially when the first semester is so packed with holidays. Fact is, these sessions prevent the whole course from being derailed/feeling shortchanged. (Side note: of course there are always students who are seriously incapacitated during storms, but I feel like [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] ganun. [REDACTED_NAME]).
